4.commentary:
Another uninteresting game for Extinction, falling in - turns to the superior Fire Drilled. 

Extinction took care of a Ball Lightning and a Viashino Sandstalker but didn't have an answer for a Granite Gargoyle. The Gargoyle took them out a few turns later.

Can't tell if the Thrulls won this or Doldrums lose it. Either way, Thrulls stay alive and advance into the second round of the qualifier bracket on the heels of some nifty summon-work for two Hecatomb's and a Kormus Bell. No creatures do damage for the Thrulls in this one, which says a lot about their capabilities at this point. 

The Doldrums are finally out of their misery! Seyonara Doldrums!

Only lost two lands during the game, was very fast out the gate and won due to mana-giver Fyndhorn Elder to summon Force of Nature. Bad Ass Fault had some defense in a Rukh and their Two-Headed Giant, and two Shockers but it wasn't enough.

What a game. First of all, the Commissioner was extremely distracted by a terrible TV show and it may have had an impact on the outcome. Crusaders had this game wrapped up until the Slivers lasted on two Coat of Arms. I'm not sure if there was a way for them to win this one but it came down to the wire.

Close game in this one. Back and forth they went, the Thrulls trying to stay under 4 cards in their hand as NLFY eagerly waited for their 4, yes FOUR, Black Vise's to wring their opponents' neck. The Thrulls held tight, did well to summon enough attack to bring NLFY down to only 5-points before it was over.

Ugly game, with both teams struggling to find any mana. In fact, both teams were discarding for the first 6 or 7 turns. FD pulls 3 more mountains, continues to hit Extinction with direct damage, and eventually ended it with a Fireball. 

Extinction really drops the ball in this one.

Thrulls by a thread. They would have lost on the very next turn, as only 3 cards remained in their library. Hard to tell if Lake of the Dead was being used properly since MVP Hecatomb needs swamps but it worked out nonetheless. 

Doldrums tossed an extra Howling Mine early in on favor of counters which may have hurt them. Can't wait to stop playing with this deck.

Thrulls go down in a sweep. Unfair to them, they're in the middle of a change in roster focus so it started to feel, in both losses, like they're were several unnecessary cards in their hand. Need to go full change to give them any chance at success. 

Well, here we are, the Drillers make it out of the Qualifier Bracket unscathed, winning 4 games in a row. These four games made it obvious to me that they can no longer win games like they used to - relying on Mana Flare to blast the doors off of opponents. Not sure they have enough protection to beat other teams, but we'll find out in their series against Darwin.

Still not enough counters for The Doldrums, and Team 10 benefits as a result. Seems like their may need to be a wall or two in place of other L-Kill cards (looking at your, Jester's Cap). Nice win for Team 10 to capitalize on a game they should have won.

Tough loss for the Throttlers. Had 3 Swamps and a Lake of the Dead, with two Dark Ritual's and yet ended up mana constrained. Might not be playing them right yet. Had a Breeding Pit in play with some heft in hand (Lord of the Pit, Ebon Praetor, and Barrow Ghoul) - feel like they should have been much more competitive here. 

Crusaders win entirely due to Kjeldoran Outpost and two Crusades. Nice win.

Awesome game by the new Things. Their game plan execution was perfect. The only critique might be their creature base - could use 2-3 more bruisers. 

BAF was right there in this one. They sprung for a Wheel of Fortune in turn 9, which put their winning move on the table in Shocker, but it also enabled the game winning move by the Things. Like the moves by BAF though.

Crusade loaded with defense in this one - 3 Crusade and 2 Castle - paired nicely with an eradication of mana. One creature would be all it took for Crusade to scorch Team 10, and that's precisely what happened.
Count it, one more win for the Things on the heels of Living Death. What can the Slivers do? Nothing.

Crusaders take the first game on the backs of an Armageddon and a Balance, which knocked out a glut of lands for NLFY. No Landia couldn't rebound well enough to win this, but took the 5-seed down to 1 point. Couldn't get much closer than this.

Wow, the Things win in 5-turns due to the pre-scribed Sliver antidote; Living Death. They throw their ferocious threesome of Spirit of the Night, Necrosavant and Polar Kraken onto the battlefield while dispatching of two Muscle Slivers and a Metallic Sliver. The next turn, a whopping 17-damage. The following turn, 22-damage.
 Gadzooks.

That's as demonstrative as it gets, as the Fault put a stamp on the series with a 43-point victory. This is what can happens when two Shockers are left alone with a Megrim on the board AND a big hand. In fact, 22-points of damage was done in the last turn alone. 

Bad Ass Fault is good.

Typical game by the Slivers - throwing out five Slivers quickly to attack their opponent. The Muscle Sliver upped their damage per turn, which was huge in disposing of the Things in 7-turns.

The Things were a bit stranded from the get-go; two islands in their initial hand with five black cards. They then drew two more islands before their first swamp, and by that time, they were toast.

Crusaders won in unusual fashion, relying on an Icatian Town, evading the Black Vise on the board and cruising to victory. 

No Landia is in trouble. They are just not built to beat this team.

Huge stay-alive win in this one. NLFY uses a Demonic Tutor to draw a Nevinrryal's Disk and consequently save their asses. Two White Knights and a Crusade are dismissed on the next turn, then a Cyclopean Tomb destabilizes the opposing mana supply. A Black Vise and Nightmare do the rest.

Darwin didn't look like they had much early on, then dealt 7-damage with a Giant Ants. They then built on the FD Mana Flare with a Hurricane to win the game and the series. 

The Drillers get knocked out by Darwin for the second season in a row. They've got a roster that can no longer compete in a tougher league. They need a fresh look.

One of the most high-suspense games we've ever seen. On the 7th turn for Bad Ass Fault, they were already down to 2-life. Team 10 had worn them down via Carnophage mayhem, even opting to trade damage with a Shocker on the other side which meant constant hand reshuffling. Finally, though, BAF drew their second Swamp and everything turned around. The next few turns showed a Hidden Horror, a Rukh, and  Rukh Egg. They sat idly by just waiting for the right time to strike as Team 10 held off their advances with a Puppet Strings. Seven more turns later, and BAF drew their favorite Pox and won the game by as thin a margin as you'll see; 1 to -1.

Critical to mention that the Fault destroyed several key lands down the stretch, eliminating a chance for Titania's Song to enter the battlefield. If she does, this game may have ended in Team 10s favor.

Team 10 is pissed. There were two games played in this one - the first one cancelled due to scorer error. I was not paying enough attention to where the damage was being doled out each turn, and this only occurred to me once the game was over. Unfortunately for Team 10, it looked like they could have had a shot in the first one.

The second game was a lopsided affair, with BAF seeing next to no resistance as they destroyed mana and the Team 10 hand turn after turn.
Second game in a row that Living Death changes the outlook of the game. It wouldn't have been possible had a Dark Ritual not enabled the summon. Next, a Polar Kraken stomps the Slivers out.

Darwin punches first with their MVP Ants. FD had already used two direct damage cards (Bolt & DIsintegrate) to (a) deal damage and (b) kill off an early Elvish Archers. They had some heft on the board to fight off the Ants and an Elvish Ranger, but their Gargoyle and Shivan Dragon weren't enough to stop the assault, not when a Giant Growth ended the game with six-damage from their Ants.

Darwin leads 1-0

Gutsy win by the Slivers, who pull the right card at the right time in Coat of Arms, putting two of them into the battlefield for three puny Slivers to turn into monsters. A 6/6 Nightmare and Demonic Hordes were suddenly outmatched, and suddenly feeling their first loss of the season.

Great win by Crusaders that they do not get without Serra's Angel. The Angel was critical in extinguishing the Doom who were left without any adequate defense to withstand her attack. 

This might have been the perfect villain to battle Darwin - the combination of fast creatures with staunch defense against any opposing attack is making it extremely difficult for Darwin to gather any momentum in this series.

Very unconventional win for the Things, as they deal with mana issues to discard a Deep Spawn on their second turn only to bring him back out two turns later. 

The Fault struggled with mana early on in this one, and, as a result, they fell behind. Two M-Kill cards in hand were left stranded, as were two Fissure's that could have handled the Deep Spawn. First loss in an incredible 16-games. Great run for the Fault.

And that's it! The Crusaders advance to their very first Finals birth behind two Armageddon's. In the process, they drown out a struggling Darwin while also gaining 16-points in the process, thus boosting them to the 36-point win. A Wild Aesthir took care of things on the ground as Darwin failed to match them with any flying defense. 

Darwin's speed was absent all series long. Games took an average of 12-turns, which is 50% longer than games Darwin won during the regular season -- not a pace they could withstand, clearly.

HUGE win by the Things to stay alive. An early Counterspell changed the entire game in their favor. They stole a Hypnotic Specter, then revived a Deep Spawn and put this one out of reach for the Fault.

Gutsy win by Darwin's Doom. They made some noise early, and then met some resistance. In fact, the tide was turning in Crusade's favor until one burly Force of Nature showed up. Crusade tried to nullify his impact by destroying all mana, but it wasn't enough to keep him from decimating their defenses.

Darwin stays alive to force a Game 6.

Thrulls first win of the season was a result of patience. They didn't play out of necessity, instead out of respect - respect in knowing that disruption wasn't necessarily a good thing. Nice win by the Thrulls. 

BAF loses again. Might an Imbalance of mountains to swamps in this deck.

GREAT win by Crusaders! They use a Cataclysm and Armageddon to perfection, killing off any hope that Darwin has to compete. The Cataclysm in the 7th turn removed an Elvish Archers, a Force of Nature and an Elvish Ranger from the field, along with 6 forests. The Crusaders were then left with a Serra Angel accompanied by a Crusade to stomp out the remaining Killer Bees. A nice, nice win.

What a battle. By far the longest game of all time at 51-turns total. The Swamp Things looked primed for victory as they stood with a Spirit of the Night, a Baron Sengir, a Commander Greven il-Vec and a Polar Kraken. The next turn, the Fault summoned a board clearing Jokulhaups. Two Nether Spirits entered for the Things, and did some damage, but it wasn't enough for two Hidden Horror's and a game-winning Pox which ended it. 

What a game.

Strong win by Crusaders to take a two games to nothing lead. They turned the tide in this one by playing an Armageddon to extinguish Darwin's 5 mana producers. Darwin staged a comeback, throwing a Woolly Spider and a Ernham Djinn out to battle, but it wasn't enough once a Crusade hit the battlefield, as it turned eight 0/1 soldiers to life for the final 14-damage.

Unusual game for both teams. Darwin finds a way to win in an extended game - this rarely happens, as they're usually toast after the 10-turn mark.

Crusade, on the other hand, has a Armageddon backfire on them as they failed to draw any more mana beyond 1 plains after the spell.

BAF turn sequence = kill off opposing swamp, remove two cards from their hand, play shocker, source demonic tutor from hand, play megrim, attack with shocker to 15-damage, remove opposing creature from battlefield, attack with shocker for another 15-damage. Whoa.

Things are not stuck when they have no lands, because of their ability to discard creatures into the graveyard. They may benefit from having two more Nether Spirits in here, as it's incredibly helpful to have them moving in and out of the battlefield without the use of mana.

Wild game won by Darwin. They deal 60 total damage in this one after having to deal with two Illusions of Grandeur's in play. It kept the Things alive for a few more turns waiting to pull a Lich or Living Death, but failed to do so. Great, impressive win by Darwin.

W.O.W. Bad Fault advances to their first Finals in their inaugural season in dramatic fashion by absolutely pulverizing the Swamp Things by an all-time record 46 points - a truly absurd margin of victory. Here's how they did it:

- Shocker = 30-damage
- Wheel of Fortune = 14-damage
- Mind Twist - 2-damage

The Megrim that made it all possible was picked up by a turn-2 Demonic Tutor, of course. What a ridiculous game.

Nice win by the Fault. They had a beautiful hand to start, with a Wheel of Fortune, a Pox, a Demonic Tutor and a Megrim. Really, the pillars of the deck. Their first ten damage was dealt by a Wheel of Fortune / Megrim combo, and then capped off by a Hymn to Tourach for another 8-damage, and a Rukh for the final 4. 

Swamp Things lost some key mana producers in this one, though there main error came after the WoF when they opted to play one more Buried Alive in anticipation of using their Living Death on the next play. Welp, they didn't get to that next turn AND risked losing their potential game-winning Living Death at the hands of more H-Kill (Hymn to Tourach).

Takes a second in a row by withstanding a serious defensive onslaught by the Fault. In six of their first seven turns, the Fault either destroyed a land or removed cards from the Crusader hand. A total of five lands were included in that dismissal, yet the Crusaders still managed 3 more lands to lay out an attack led by a White Knight, and followed by Wild Aunt Esther, and a Soul Warden. The Fault has done defense in a Rukh and a Hyppy but it wasn't enough with the MVP-Crusade in play. 

Gutsy win by Crusade.

Battle of the Top Dogs! Darwin pulls a great hand of offense and defense, is able to put away the only two Slivers to make it onto the battlefield, then took down their opponent with a trio of Fyndy Elf, Elder and Ernham Djinn. A Force of Nature and Elvish Ranger in-hand provided some nice backup too.

Slivers had strong mana supply but their spells didn't match the mana colors. Rematch will be fun in Week 14!

Crusaders drew four mana in this one, though three of them were destroyed by the Fault. A mid- game Demonic Tutor gave way to a game-ending Wheel of Fortune. Two Megrim were on the board, this dealing the 28-damage to seal it.

Big, big win by the Crusaders. They out-mana their opponent and take down the #2-seed with familiar attackers in White Knight and Wild Aunt Esther. 

The Fault look lackluster for a second straight game, and have now fallen behind 3-1. It's back against the wall time!

Welp, found out where all the lands were hiding for the Drillers. Their fourth loss in a row, unprecedented for them. I feel a bounce back coming.. 

A game Extinction badly needed. With FD fumbling, the X squad rightfully took advantage and closed things quickly.

Weird game, neither side had any offense for the first half of the game. The Thrulls, in a way, were being handed a chance at beating one of Season 4's top teams but they could not. They perplex me still.
A blitzing pace turns Game 1 into the Fault's first finals victory, after decimating the Crusaders with an offensive/defensive show.
Crusaders even the series after a Jokulhaups goes wrong for the Fault. They were back out of the gate with too much speed for the Fault to contend.

Victory!! The Crusaders take it home in game 6! They out drew the Fault for mana and took advantage, deploying a Wild Aunt Esther, a White Knight and two Soldier Tokens. Much of the victory is in part to Land Tax, who took home the MVP. 

The Fault meet their match, losing more games in the finals than in their previous 20 games.

NLFYs struggles continue in this one despite their opponent having only 2 lands for most of the game. They have only 6 attacking creatures and still have no way to dismantle black creatures. Need to figure this out or they're in big trouble.

Team 10 wins a close one here - maybe not in score, but in gameplay. Their 2 lands would normally spell trouble against NLFY, but they managed to survive. Great complementary flow between Yawgmoth Demon and Puppet Strings in this one with Sol Ring paying instant dividends (just purchased).

NLFY: Second turn, a Demonic Tutor was used to pull a Nevinrryal's Disk, and what a move that ended up being - it killed a Spirit of the Night and the Things Null Brooch, giving NLFY some daylight as the crowd headed for the exit. The next turn, Demonic Hordes took over, destroying 4 lands. The Things hand grew, which meant Black Vise joined in on the damage party, and ended the game. A great win by NLFY.

Elvish Archers keeps both a River Boa and a Savanah Lions at bay for much of the game. Darwin reaches critical mana mass to start throwing punches, and X sputters as they stand frozen with only two lands for much of the game. Still, X has a chance with one more mana. They felt strong in this one somehow.

Big win for Crusade as they rely on the flight of Wild Aesthir to break down the Things. One more swamp changes this game though, as the Things sat on a Living Death but couldn't summon it. Good win, C-II.

Great win by NLFY who calls up Cyclopean Tomb on their second turn via Demonic Tutor. The Tomb ends up turning every one of X's 9 lands into Swamps, rendering them absolutely frozen in place. There were 3 Black Vise's and a Nevinrryal's Disk on the battlefield too. A slow but precise beat down from NLFY.

HUGE win by Bad Ass Fault. They're on the brink of destruction, with 2-life left, and hung on looking/waiting for the right card. They drew one more key land destruction card which kept a Yawgmoth Demon off the battlefield, then the MVP-Jokulhaups, which turned their Rukh Egg's into 2 mighty Rukh's. Two turns later, it was all over.

Alright! Proof in the pudding that Extinction can win a game :) They take down a top-dog in Swamp Things. They had one more Wrath of God in hand too so even a swarm of Grave-Res for the Things may not have changed anything. Good win by X.

The Things needed one more swamp to get themselves off the ground. It's possible that there's an imbalance of mana w/r/t summon spells x color.

The most focused I've ever had to be to rightfully manage a game. The Thrulls had to be diligent in their mana use as they shifted from summoning power to Lake of the Dead sacrifices. The Slivers, on the other hand, had all the mana they could ask for but one; an island. They were able to survive longer than expected due to Victual Sliver (sacrificing Slivers pre-damage in battle) to gain life, while also sustaining an attack on the other end. A gutsy, great win by the Thrulls.

Exhausting game - took me an hour to complete, but think it was played as it should have been.

Can't tell if this was a win by NLFY or a loss by Doldrums. Doldrums put out a Howling Mine and it backfired. NLFY used Black Vise to gauge a counterspell with other summon spells, and it worked. They were able to summon two of them, and that put a serious threat on Doldrums. Their hand accelerated, they played anything they could, like a Aether Storm, and that backfired too - couldn't summon creatures to get them out of their hand. NLFY was able to get a Cyclopean Tomb out also, and immediately turned the majority of Doldrums' 7 mana into Swamps, nullifying their summoning power even more.

Great win by Extinction. They were in a big hole early, falling victim to a Megrim-Cursed Rack combination. Summoning a Deadly insect was critical to their survival/victory.

BAF had all the mana in the world to kill X with a Mind Twist or even another Hymn to Tourach after playing 3 Megrim's. Neither card came, and they wilted under the pressure of Deadly Insect. 

Solid game by both Teams making me believe in both of their win-con strategies.

Finally a win by the Drillers, and a big one at that taking down the #1-seeded Doom. An ample mana supply fueled their attack, winning on the 7th turn by taking advantage of a Furnace of Rath to deal double damage from Incinerate and Disintegrate. 

Darwin had a great draw, had just summoned a Yavimaya Ants to partner with Woolly Spider on the battlefield. They had enough mana out to lay down a Stream of Life or a Force of Nature on their next turn. Hindsight bias says they should have used their L-Pump early, but they were right there.

Doldrums use 2 Grindstone's to eliminate 14 cards in one play, which set the stage for their win. That was the game-changing round - half the Thrull deck was gone, and they were left with nothing two rounds later.

Thrulls had too many C-Kill cards in this one, which hurt their ability to do other interesting things against a creature-light outfit in Doldrums. 

Overall, good win by The Doldrums.

Slivers trot out four creatures and two Coat of Arms' in this one - meaning an automatic +6/6 on the board for each Sliver - whoa. Only way to defend against that is with excessive C-Kill or A-Kill. Team 10 had neither.

Team 10 had a Yawgmoth Demon, Wormwood Treefolk, Sengir and Carnophage on the board, but stood no chance against the suddenly mighty Slivers.

With a Black Vise in play, Crusade decides to use an Armageddon hoping to nullify any additional advances by NLFY while relying on their access of mana from Land Tax. This strategy, while a bold one, failed completely. They were unable to summon several large-CC cards in hand (2 Icatian Towns, 2 Armageddon, 1 Dingus Egg) and were left for dead by the Vise.

NLFY had a Demonic Horde and Nightmare ready to hit the battlefield when the Armageddon was summoned. Either way, it didn't look like Crusaders had a chance in this one.

Holy fast, flying, muscled, metallic slivers batman!!! A turn-4 win ties a league record previously set by Darwin (Season 1) and your Slivers (Season 2). Insanely fast. Three straight dual-lands set the tone, and the Slivers couldn't look back they were surging so quickly forward. It also keep the Slivers tied for 1st place with Darwin as we meet the half-point in the season.

Well, Swamp Things would have liked this one, but they weren't even close. No early momentum after a Buried Alive was played on their third turn. Losing in 4-turns is absurd, and very few teams can prevent that from happening at all.

Darwin gets so much mana out so fast with Gaea's Touch, then a Fyndhorn Elder & Elf that they didn't even need to use. Nonetheless, they throw two Ernham Djinn's, a Woolly Spider and then a Force of Nature for good measure. The Djinn's did the dirty work as Team 10 struggled to put anything in play. Also a critical game to get some separation from the Slivers, who have a tough match-up with the Things later this week.

Team 10 didn't have any forests, and they needed 'em, or at least one, just to get their offense going. Hopefully an anomaly.

Critical poor-play by FD on their third turn to use their three mana for a Viashino Sandstalker instead of a Wheel of Fortune -- they had no more mana in hand and had to know  their lands would go away soon. 

NLFY goes right to work in this one, destroying 2 early mountains with Choking Sands, then turning three more into swamps courtesy of Cyclopean Tomb and two Evil Presence's. Two Black Vise's took care of the rest.

Well, these two teams aren't very good. That's my conclusion after this record breaking 21-turn game. Yikes, good thing Jenny had an extra dose of patience this morning :)

It feels like the artifact-to-life concept for Extinction isn't real - haven't seen it play out once this season. Wonder why that is - either way, feel like they need to move closer to it, or farther away. They're in no man's land right now.

The Frottle are as they are labeled to be; pathetic. Sacrificing creatures, lands and any advantage they ever have for little gain. NO idea what to do with them but one thing is for certain - they stink.

Slivers continue to roll - seven Slivers summoned in this one, including a Queen (who dealt the last bit of damage to end the game). 

Drillers' comeback feels lost now in what was a disspiriting game. They had strong enough mana in this one, killed off two Slivers early, then took out for Slivers with a Heart of Bogardan to reset the board. Of course, the Slivers had drawn a City of Brass to play a Sliver Queen the previous turn, and there was no way to get rid of her. Scratch that play, and the Drillers very well could have made this a game.

Wow. Bad Ass Fault's strategy comes together in 7 quick turns. Two Rain of Tears destroy NLFY early mana pool, then took a few turns to put a Megrim, Shocker, Rukh Egg, and Hypnotic Specter. By the 7th turn, NLFY had only two swamps and a Junun Efreet, playing defense hoping for some miracle cards to show up.

Bad Ass Fault then throws down the game-changing card - Pox - which kills Junun Efreet, takes out 2 cards in NLFYs hand, and blasts one of their swamps and deals 7 damage to put them at 13. BAF loses their Rukh Egg for a Rukh, then attacks with all three creatures to deal the remaining 19 damage - a big-time move by the Fault.

Don't know that Darwin ever felt truly compromised in this one. They threw two Ernham Djinn's, a Fyndhorn Elder and a Killer Bee's to battle the Crusaders. The Crusaders, however, only managed two pesky Soul Warden's in defense. Just not enough to hold off a #1 seed.

Oh yeah, the trade for Titania's Song pays immediate dividends as it takes the game MVP award in the first outing. Two Ivory Tower's, a Diamond Kaleidoscope and a Puppet Strings attack the Thrulls head on, and end things two turns later with the help of a 6-damage Hurricane. 

The Thrulls sputter with mana issues, and manage only a Breeding Pit and one token in this one.

Well well, Extinction finally shows some life, though late in the game, and still falls in defeat to a NLFY team who summoned only two creatures all game. 

Extinction: In successive turns, Extinction plays two artifacts, a Wrath of God, then one more artifact and Titania's Song to wage an all-out-artifact offensive against NLFY. It would have worked, except NLFY summoned a Nightmare after the WoG, then blocked all three artifacts with the Nightmare and their two 1/1 Black Vise's. NLFY ends things on the next turn with their Nightmare, leaving Extinction just exasperated.

NLFY held tightly to a Icy Manipulator early on, deflating any attack from X.

Thin margin of victory for Swamp Things who rely on a critical Recurring Nightmare swap of Hidden Horror for Spirit of the Night (which was discarded by the Horror, no less). Later in the game, the Things used an Arcane Denial to counter a Zuran Orb that could've elongated the game for Crusade.

Big victory by Swamp Things.

A well executed game by Bad Ass Fault as they ride an early Entropic Specter / Megrim combination to deal most of their damage. The Specter was the only creature summoned by Bad Ass Fault. BAF drew two HUGE cards at the end - Stone Rain & Fissure - to destroy any chance the Drillers had at winning.

FD loses yet again in a tight contest. They win this game if the two mountains weren't destroyed near the end.

Darwin's core game plan is a natural antidote to the counter strategy of Doldrums. In this one, they rely on a Sol Ring, a Fyndhorn Elder and a Wild Growth to summon multiple spells per turn as they sidestep their opponents' massive defense. A City of Solitude came at the right time, blocking a Evacuation and, as a result, winning the game for Darwin.

Armageddon Clock hurts more than it helps in this one. Team 10 thought they'd be able to withstand any damage with their twin Ivory Tower's in play but no additional help came. They had one last trick up their sleeve to win the game (Yawgmoth Demon + Instill Energy) but their Sol Ring was disposed of by MVP Disenchant.

Rock Hydra may have only five 4 damage in this game as a 1/1, but they forced The Doldrums to think twice on every turn. Drillers win a scrappy one. 

Doldrums need to be dismantled. They're no fun to play with, and have a very weak strategy.

Wild game. The Fault couldn't find a creature to help block two measly Thrulls. A Entropic Specter didn't help much either given the H-Kill - helping prove out the fact that the Specter doesn't really mesh in this group. 

A late Jokulhaups cleared the board of 4 more swamps (10 in the game) and a Lord of the Pit, which would have won the Thrulls the game on their next turn.

Smoked. The Slivers take care of business quickly in this one, despite having only 3-mana. Two Muscle Sliver's do most of the damage but are joined by Acidic and Metallic Slivers. 

NLFY had two Royal Assassin's in hand, along with an Icy Manipulator and a Nevinrryal's Disk. One more Swamp changes this game dramatically.

What a great win by Team 10. They weathered some early mana / Black Vise problems, then took down a 4/4 Nightmare with Hurricane. This put them in the hole fairly big early, having not dealt any damage to NLFY yet. 

They then go on the offensive with a Ernham Djinn and Carnophage as Puppet Strings nullifies the opposing Assassin. NLFY then clears the board with a Nevinyrral's Disk and T-10 responds with a Treefolk, a revived Nightmare and puts the game away.

More revenge for the Drillers as they get their second win in a row against Extinction. They threw a lot at X, and most of it was nullified either by Reverse Damage's, Disenchant or otherwise. 

Even with 4-life left, a win is a win.

Mana acceleration gets a Elvish Archer and a Yavimaya Ants into the battlefield and Team 10 couldn't answer. Three mana left T-10 with only two Carnophage to handle the onslaught while taking damage all the while

Extinction stinks. They're made to kill opposing creatures but can't seem to do so with any level of effectiveness. 

Crusade was never challenged in this one, easy win.

Great game by Bad Ass Fault. They use several mana kill cards to freeze Extinction, then throw a Pox out for good measure. Doing so put a Rukh on the battlefield, and soon ended things for X. 

Extinction was making their moves but couldn't hang on.

Ugly game between two bottom-feeders. Thrulls wait out the counters from Doldrums, plop their Kormus Bell on the board and slow-crawl to victory.

Doldrums have nothing -- no offense, never appears to be enough defense. Need a serious overhaul.

NLFY had this wrapped up with an Efreet on the battlefield, a lot mana in play, and a Nightmare, Dark ritual and Drain Life in hand. A total misread by me for choosing the least damaging player (Efreet) to play on instead of the Nightmare, but then it wouldn't have mattered anyway. 

Armageddon continues to flatten NLFY.

High expectations for this game were tempered once FD were hit with severe mana issues. 

Darwin were able to play two Yavimaya Ants and a Fallow Worm before FD even put a creature in play. They wore down their opponent and won handily.

The Drillers had a strong hand w/ two Hydras, a Ball Lightning and a few others to start the game - but only one Mountain. They were stuck with that one mana producer for more than half of the game. A late-game Mana Flare could have changed things, but Darwin was equipped with one of their two Desert Twisters and immediately took it out. Would have been a big win for the Drillers.

Very, very nice win for Team 10. They're starting to look like a 'team'!!!  4 Artifacts, two Birds of Paradise, a Ernham Djinn, a Royal, and a Yawgmoth Demon enabled a strong onslaught against the fearsome Slivers.

Slivers had an Island, two City of Brass and four slivers they could toss out, but it wasn't enough to overthrow Team 10. They also were left stranded late in the game with 3 Coat of Arms in hand alongside a Sliver Queen, but couldn't summon them.

Big win for No Landia as they get a bit of separation from the middle-quartet of the league,including Swamp Things. 

NLFY had a great draw in this one; ton of mana, two early M-Kill cards then two Icy Manipulator's to stall the opponent for the remainder of the game. A 9/9 Nightmare late in the game helped Junun Efreet to close things out easily. 

Swamp Things lose 4 critical mana in this one despite a hand full of graveyard resurrection cards. Tough loss.

Destruction by the #2 Slivers. Six turns meant six lands and six Slivers, each with haste, flying and first strike. One of those six was a Queen. Good thing they drew a disenchant to dispel the Icy Manipulator on Doldrums, who had a Mask and a silent Dreadnought in play, though it may not have mattered. 

Doldrums are, well, stuck in the doldrums.

Maybe the best game of the season. Both teams traded punches, momentum shifted maybe 7 different times, and Darwin wins by one point. Wow. 

Darwin used two big Desert Twisters, two Forces of Nature and a few others to knock X back. Then a Killer Bee's helped take out a Deadly Insect that would have ended things one turn later. 

Extinction showed something in this one, hanging with the #1 squad for a 17-turn dog fight. Regeneration was a tough mechanic for Darwin to deal with too.

A total beat down by the 3-seed on the 5-seed. Sure, the Crusaders only had one Plains drawn in this game, but more mana may not have mattered, as Bad Ass Fault had three M-Kill cards in hand. Either way, two Shockers sit and wait for a Megrim that comes on turn-7, then train 28 damage down in their lowly opponent.

Definitely helps the Fault put some pressure on the sliding No Landia as we move closer to the playoffs.

Nice win by Team 10. Took a gamble by throwing a Titania's Song onto the battlefield so early, as it could have acted as a potentially stong defensive move later in the game. Nonetheless, two Carnophage, an Ivory Tower and a Time Bomb dealt enough damage to secure the win later on in the game. 

Swamp Things just couldn't get the right creatures in play despite their 7-mana -- they were all in the graveyard!!! As planned, I might add. Interesting dynamic, and will need to mess with a few of the mechanics in this deck (See: Lich). Good game though.

Another big win for the Drillers. They looked like their old selves in this one - winning in 6 quick turns with just the right amount of firepower. This game puts them now tied with Extinction for 9th place, and 1.5 games back of the Thrulls. Here they come...

The Slivers didn't quite have the right mana combination to stave off FD int his one. One more Island may have helped, but they had but one Counterspell in hand, so "who could know"?

Good grief. The Fault totally dismantle their near-kin opponent with 25 pts of damage on turn-7. One Shocker and two Megrim did the trick to close this one out in demonstrative fashion.

NLFY were beat partially by their own game - having two early mana destroyed by a Hymn to Tourach and Rain of Tears. They couldn't rebound, didn't draw any additional mana, and were left to rot.

Big playoff indications in this one, as The Fault increase their lead on NLFY to 1.5 games.

FD seemingly had this game in their back pocket, having dealt 14 damage early as Crusade scrambled to keep up. It all changed with a Armageddon / Zuran Orb combination that revived the Crusaders, froze the Drillers and cemented victory.

Good win for Crusaders who continue to battle for a top-4 seed in the playoffs and are currently 1-game back from NLFY.

Welp, the 9 new FAs that were just signed by the Thrulls looks like, ya know, a pretty good call. Thrulls toss a Sengir Autocrat and Hecatomb out early, take out 4 slivers immediately, and start to wreak havoc until a disenchant cuts them off. Both teams stall a bit, losing mana due to the Desolation (fun card) until Slivers play a Muscle Sliver and Coat of Arms in preparation for more slivers to come. 

What happens? The Thrulls play a Lord of the Pit, then immediately sacrifice him for the same Sengir Autocrat from earlier, which gives three more Serf tokens in play to benefit from the same Coat of Arms. 

Theeeeeeeee Thrulls win!!!

Bad Ass Fault is starting to look scary good. They just dismantle the Swamp Things in this one, destroying two early lands, and ready to kill off a third at any time. Strip Mine helpful in its' first appearance. Once Megrim entered the battlefield alongside Shocker, this game was over.

The Things are lacking speed - maybe that's their biggest issue. Would a few more M-Pump cards turn their fates? Maybe.

Brutal win for Team 10. It was coming down to the wire, and Doldrums had to make a few critical decisions near the end of the game including a Braingeyser on their own deck hoping for some defense that didn't come.

Team 10 rides a revived Yawgmoth Demon to victory. An earlier Demon was stolen by Doldrums, yet destroyed with a Tranquility very quickly - big help from new free agent.

Doldrums also looked better with their new FAs; Control Magic, Clone and Vesuvan.

Definitely a win for Crusaders vs. a loss for the Thrulls. The Crusaders are finding that an early-game Armageddon is critical to their chances at winning. They just don't need mana the way other teams do. Nicely played by them.

The Thrulls were close here, once again showing the Autocrat/Hecatomb combination. The Armageddon ruined their chances though, as they struggled to find one more creature to put in play - the 3 Lord's of the Pit didn't help bc they couldn't summon them.

INCREDIBLE win by No Landia. They win by 3 points after being down by 19. Here's how it happened:

Darwin -- Two quick M-Pumps allow for an Ernham Djinn, a Killer Bees and a Yavimaya Ants to knock NLFY down from 20 to 1. 

NLFY, in response, uses a Ashes to Ashes to dispose of the Ernham and K-Bee, then destroys 3 of their 4 lands, which disables the Ants. They then cast a Worms of the Earth for good measure, now with 6+ swamps of their own. A Demonic Hordes hit the battlefield and continues to destroy 3 more forests as they make it onto the battlefield. Darwin is helpless, and NLFY adds a Junun Efreet to come ALL THE WAY BACK to defeat the #1-seed by 3 points. 

What a game.

Yet another game where Extinction struggles to kill off any of the opposing creatures. Somewhat odd for this team, though the main issue in this one was mana. They had only two for the game, and could've used at least one Repentance, though their two Reprisal's wouldn't have helped.

Slivers win a must-win game against a bottom-3 opponent. Bad Ass Fault is on a roll, and gunning for that #2-seed over the Lining.

Close one. Unclear if Doldrums could have won this game with better card-play. Felt like there were a few moves down the stretch that could have saved them - i.e. laying 4 mana for a Clone instead of sticking to the counter-game. 

Swamp Things use some pesky grave-res abilities by Nether Shadow and Necrosavant to circumvent the Counter strategy by Doldrums. Good game.

What a game!

A turn-5 Demonic Tutor brings a Demonic Hordes onto the board. The Hordes ends up destroying 6 of the Thrulls ten mana, with the other four destroyed by M-Kill cards. The Thrulls did what they could to contend in the air, summoning a Lord of the Pit aiming to out-heft NLFY, but a Icy Manipulator totally diffused the big boy.

The game sits for 21 total turns, longest of the year, as NLFY waits out their opponent in patient fashion.

Ugly game where both teams struggled with mana early on. Of course, Darwin's mana issues didn't last for long, responding with two Fyndhorn Elder's who unleashed the MVP Yavimaya Ants. The Ants were responsible for the first 15 damage against the Thrulls. The last 5 were dealt by a Hurricane for a clean 15-0 win.

Darwin's Doom secures their first-ever #1-seed with this victory. The Thrulls can't be too downtrodden as they lost entirely due to mana issues.

Savvy win by the Swamp Things. They disable FDs creature offense/defense with a Living Death even though it didn't resurrect anything for them. They then show a Dream Halls, cast a Polar Kraken for the first time, and proceed to win two turns later. A nice win by two newcomers for the Things.

FD was beat up here. They have no defense against an enchantment which is a big deal. The league has gotten stronger by including powerful enchantments and the Drillers are powerless against them. They'll need an upgrade here to ensure they don't continue to fall behind.

Excellent win by the Fault. Two early Hymn to Tourach's helped knock out most of the Slivers' hand early. Then a Hyppy removed two critical creatures in SLs hand. That was the game-changer, as the Slivers were a sinking ship from then on. 

The Fault win by attacking with Hidden Horror, then taking advantage of a Pox/Megrim to seal it. Nicely done to contend for the #1-seed in their very first season by de-throning the previous regular season champ.

5.commentary:
Stiffs. Nice win by the new team, relying on a Birds of Paradise, Winter Orb, Entropic Specter and Wormwood Treefolk to take down Murphy's Law. Winter Orb was the winner here, as it ensured that the Law wouldn't summon anything to contend. Meekstone in-hand ready for combat too.

Law. Tough one, they needed several turns to put even three lands in play, and by that time they were on death's doorstep.

The Fault destroyed five lands via Rain of Tears, Stone Rain and Pox. With Pox, they put a Rukh into play who deals most of the remaining damage. One Hymn to Tourach helped out with four damage via Megrim.

FD gets beat here, but it wasn't a hopeless game. They had a ton of mana, put a Mana Flare in play and would have remained competitive if the Fault hadn't destroyed so many of their lands. They handled three opposing creatures with direct damage spells, and generally looked ready to compete. Nice.

Potatoes. Good win against new foe; Icy Hot. They use two Crusade's to pump up a Tundra Wolves, Wild Aesthir, Longbow Archer and Kjeldoran Guard to win the game handily. Two pump ups were huge. Key here, also, was the the Potatoes summoned 10 cards, TEN, with only 3 mana (and help from a Sol Ring). That's impressive.

Hot. Well, they're fun! Pyroclasm is a great card against most teams in this league, and they would have totally thwarted the Potato offense if not for two Crusade's. Will need to learn how to play with them on, mainly, when to do damage and when to kill creatures. Very fun though.

Darwin - Good win here, and a game that they should have won. The game puttered along until the Killer Bees showed up and knocked out the fly-less Attritionists.

Attritionists - three things worked against them in this game; 1) City of Solitude disabled their chance at using Thrull Wizard's counter ability, 2) they had no flying creatures and 3) Hecatomb never showed up. If ANY of those three change, then we might be talking about how The Attritionists won their first game.

MAY have screwed NLFY in this one. Stopped counting Black Vise damage points, and accounted for 1 of them once i realized it, but there may have been one, or maybe even two, more points. My bad :)

NLFY. Junun Efreet sort of screwed them in this game, soaking up two precious mana each turn. Is Efreet's bad worse than their good?

Slivers. Win this one behind Sliver Queen's heft, destroying a Junun Efreet in the process. They could really benefit from Aluren, and I may have to figure out an appropriate trade.

Atts. In a game of defense, the less-offensively oriented won! That's not totally fair, I guess. The Atts won because of a first turn Underworld Dreams who dealt most of the damage in this one. Pushing a mid-game Hecatomb onto the battlefield helped too but was limited by the Winter Orb in play. A good victory, as I wouldn't have played U-world Dreams that early in the past I don't think. They didn't have one creature attack actually. Pretty good. Drawing 8 total lands helped too.

Stiffs. Blew it. They had all their players but were limited by mana (4). Used a Demonic Tutor on a creature, which was foolish (too easily killed). Felt like they were just sitting around waiting for the Demon to get into play and just couldn't get there bc of their own Winter Orb. Lost a defensive battle in this one.

Doom. Smart win in this one but one that ONLY came with the game-MVP Tranquility. They used not one but two of them to dispatch of Hot's Manabarbs and Mudslide in play. The first was countered. An Erhnam Djinn did the rest while a Ants, Ranger and Elf were killed. Drew all three Tranquility cards in this one - lucky!

Hot. Looked to be in control early in the game with ample mana, defense and counters in-hand. They quickly dealt out 6-damage but ran out of answers for Darwin's creature base. Very dependent on the Manabarbs but looks to be a promising series.

Hot. GREAT win! Love the hang tight and wait for the right cards game plan. It really caused Darwin fits in this game. Two Wall of Heat, one Wall of Tears and some counters pretty much nullified everything the Doom wanted to do (attack). Worked very nicely, and Rathi dealt 20 of 21 damage. So fun. Deck might want a creature who can counter though.

Doom. Having no C-Kill hurt them big time here. They had E-Kill and A-Kill but didn't need them. Three walls took them out of their stuff, even after destroying one Rathi. They were completely stalled. Maybe this is the end of all-offense no-defense Darwin..

Stiffs. Wow, another late-game victory for the Stiffs! Their formula for the Atts involved more Mirri and more Storm Seekers combined with game-MVP Orb. Most other cards for them were just noise outside of Tutor's. Two Storm Seeker's were used in this one, with one being used twice (Regrowth) and another being summoned via Demonic Tutor. Mirri wore them down the rest of the way. With this win, the Stiffs advance to the next round!!

Attritionists. Well, this loss marks the end of their inaugural season. They'll be back, as there were a few good pieces that seem foundational for a deck but man do they need help. The defensive play is fine, but they're all over the place. They need to double-down on one or two resources to go after, sharpen the focus and add a bit more offense as well. Their only methods of attack are via Idol's or Hecatomb and the latter is hard to get into play. Changes to come, but a fun deck when in full swing.

Doom. Well done, Doom. They win this one on their terms - in 6-turns. An early Ants led to 15-damage, then a few more came from a live Manabarbs with the finisher via Hurricane. Onto the next round they go!

Hot. Soldevi Excavations backfired in this one nullifying huge counters that could have helped stall the game. Now they have a long offseason to think about the roster, and the Excavations may not be back after this foible. Good run by the Hot and very competitive in this series. Felt like they were finding their strategy.

Darwin. Skated to victory due to Fire Drilled mana issues.

FD. Tough loss due to mana constraints, even after recycling their hand with a Winds of Change.

Stiffs. Win this one partially due to Winter Orb, but also due to poor use of Demonic Tutor. Nonetheless, they ride two Carnophage's to victory, and destroy a Royal Assassin with a Time Bomb to keep the train moving.

NLFY. Bad loss for them, as they pulled a Royal Assassin with their Demonic Tutor instead of a Nevinyrral's Disk - this cost them the game. Otherwise, couldn't get out in front of the Winter Orb to destroy lands, and the Carnophage were too fast.

BAF. Hyppy into Demonic Tutor into Megrim into Pox into Wheel of Fortune. Most all damage was done on turn-6 with their usual combo of total devastation. 

Things. Drew 3 mana in this one, with two destroyed. Otherwise, they may get a Fog Elemental or two on the board, and start their Buried Alive train.

A very, very fun game. I like both of these teams a lot and look forward to figuring out how to play with them.

ML. Were down by 20 to 6 before taking advantage of a Enlightened Tutor-into-Pandemonium with a Phyrexian Dreadnought and a Ball Lightning to deal 24-damage. Yowza, that's a lot.

Attritionists. Have something here. Underworld Dreams did 4-damage, then a few thrulls tried to do the rest. They didn't win but they brought down a beastly foe to 6-life. They also put a Bottomless Pit in play, a fun card. Hecatomb was in-hand but couldn't get it out fast enough, and didn't have the mana to really take advantage. Something here tho...

Slivers. Mana imbalance to start out, then had to be patient before summoning Crystalline Sliver. That was the winning creature.

Hot. Team would much stronger with non-land mana producers. Need to figure that part out.

Doom. Were able to withstand early sludgery of the Stiffs and overcame with their strong stable of creatures. By game's end, a Djinn, Fallow Wurm, Archers, Spider and two Elves were on the battlefield. Two Giant Growth's helped expedite the victory too, which may have been critical in bypassing any additional defense from their opponent.

Stiffs. Well, as good as they looked last round they looked equally lackluster in this one. A much different opponent in terms of build and pace and they were not setup for it. Not nearly enough efficient C-Kill or C-Freeze. Core complaint of this team is how hard it has to work to do what it wants. This was a good example.

Doom. Buh buh bum bum bump, the champ is here! Darwin makes short work of the Stiffs to win the Qualifier Bracket to advance into the Premier bracket as the final seed!! How'd they do it - using a first turn Fastbond to pump out 7 mana in 4-5 turns to overload the Stiffs' Orb. A Djinn and a Ranger did the rest! A critical 1-damage Hurricane was used to dispatch two mana-Birds on the other end, too.

Stiffs. So much work! They used 3 Worldly Tutor's to put two Birds of Paradise in play, then both were poached minutes later. Their third Tutor used on a Walking Wall but it was too late for the speed of Darwin. Thanks for the memories, Stiffs but this the end of the line for your too-slow-for-the-show ways!!

FD. Had five mana, a Fireball, two Winds of Change and a Wheel of Fortune in hand down in the game, and facing a Rathi Dragon. The decision to play Winds of Change, in an effort to gain mana + damage backfired, as it empowered Icy Hot to win.

Hot. Use a few key Counterspell's and a controversial Rathi Dragon to win this one. The Winds of Change gave them a Manabarbs, Twiddle and a few others to seal this one. Rathi Dragon doesn't fit despite winning MVP in this one.

Darwin. Fell down by 12 early due to Spirit of the Night, and would have gone on the offensive if not for the Things countering their Hurricane. 

Things. Offseason roster changes make immediate impact, with two straight Dark Ritual's one for a Buried Alive and the other for a Necromancy to put a Spirit of the Night in play on turn-2. The beast knocked out their opponent 3-turns later. Important to note that the Things won with only 2 mana in play - nice.

Stiffs. Good win for the Stiffs as they remain undefeated. They put a Entropic Specter into play on the third turn thanks to a Birds and Elf. The Specter, with a Instill Energy, is what decimated the Slivers, who struggled to defend without taking damage every turn. A few turns later, a Concordant Crossroads was played with a second Yawgmoth Demon, setting the Slivers ablaze. A Giant Growth to the Elf is what did it.

Slivers. Tough mana game, as they were stuck with only two for most of the game despite a Howling Mine being in play. No way for them to win this one.

Potatoes. Looked to be in complete control in this one. They weren't challenged at all by the Att's. Their Longbow Archer & Kjeldoran Home guard pumped by two Crusade's totally overwhelmed the Att's. They sat on a ton of defense too; Wrath of God & 3 Disenchant's.

BAF. Destroys 3 lands, then discards 5 cards from Murphy's hand. A Demonic Tutor brought a Hidden Horror & Rukh into the battlefield who took care of things in 3-consecutive turns as Murphy failed to draw mana.

Murphy. Only 3 mana in 10 turns is rough. All three were destroyed mid-game, and that left them for dead. No bueno.

Fault. A slow, intentional dismantling of Darwin. Most damage was done by Team Captain; Hypnotic Specter accompanied by a Shocker or two. A Pox made this game appear closer than it actually was. Fault only destroy one land outside of Pox. Megrim helped some as well.

Doom. Lands were all there, but didn't have any firepower to go along with it. With some creatures, this may have gone the other way. Lost a Tranquility early, which hurt them with Megrim on the board.

NLFY. Great win! They destroy 8 creatures and 7 lands in this one to take Game 1! An early Nevinyrral's Disk was critical in knocking out several creatures, a Crusade and a Field of Souls. Then, turns later, a Sengir Vampire was joined by a Demonic Hordes and the table completely turned. The Hordes chipped away at the Potatoes' mana and left them stranded. The Sengir was left unguarded by 4 Potato creatures and did the rest. 

Potatoes. Beaten fair and square despite sitting on 3 Wrath of God's, two Armageddon's and a few other spells. Tough loss, but they'll be back!

Law. Benefit from a great first draw with a Sandstalker, Angelic Renewal, a Tutor, Balance and a few lands. Grabbed a Ball Lightning over the next few turns and used the Balance to knock out a Necrosavant and a Nether Shadow to clear a path to victory. The Ball Lightning came a few turns later once Pandemonium hit, at which point 18-damage was done to lock in Game 1.

Things. Were outdrawn initially, had a lot of lands and Grave-Res and just couldn't stay competitive once the Balance knocked out their creature base.

Drillers. Great win. They matched the Slivers' full stable of creatures with direct damage discarding five creatures in the process. They dealt more damage with a Thunderbolt and Incinerate, then a bit more with a Gargoyle and a Horde once the battlefield was open to seal the deal.

Slivers. This is going to be a tough series. They couldn't get any momentum early and were constantly damaging themselves with mana usage. This is going to be a fun series.

Doom. Put a Djinn out on turn-2 and that fueled the attack all game. A timely Hurricane disposed of two Rukh's and nearly sealed the victory a few turns later. Good win by Darwin.

Fault. Needed some C-Kill in this one to stave off the Djinn attack. With two Rukh's in play and two Shocker's to hold off the Djinn, it felt as tho the momentum had shifted but the Hurricane stomped that out immediately.

NLFY. Incredible win. They were down to 16 to 1 and came alllllll the way back. Phenomenal. They used a last ditch Nevinyrral's Disk to wipe the board, played an Icy Manipulator to control the Potatoes land base, then a Black Vise, Terror, Choking Sands to kill off another land, a Royal and a Demonic Hordes to take the W. Amazing string of cards drawn in this sequence.

Potatoes. This one just got away. They were cooking early with attackers and C-Pump on the board. A Cyclopean Tomb turned four lands into Swamps and four more were destroyed. Tough loss for the Potatoes.

Law. Won this game with only two Mountain's for most of the game. A Sol Ring enabled all but 3 damage via the game MVP-Sandstalker. A Ball Lightning helped too, as did the game-ending Lightning Bolt. Out-drew their opponent in this one.

Things. Started with a lot of mana, two Kraken's and a Deep Spawn. Just a clunky mix that didn't go anywhere.

Things. Back to the basic formula, the Things lay a first-turn Buried Alive and follow it up with a Living Death a few turns later. Then, a whopping 32-damage rained down on the Law via two Kraken's, a Fog Elemental and team captain; Spirit of the Night.

Law. Had nothing here, and showed the true colors that were evident all season long. Mana struggles into no offense or defense. There's just no way this team can slow anyone down - it's their way or their doors get blown off. Not sustainable strategy.

Doom. Well, well!! The Doom are on the offensive against the #1-seed! This time, they relied heavily on a Fyndhorn Elder to launch their attack - an Ants/Archer combo did most of the damage, with a Hurricane to help finish it off. Very, very nice win by the former #1-seed.

Fault. Find themselves in odd territory down 2 games to 1. They had mana struggles early, and that stunted their defensive onslaught. A late Wheel of Fortune did little to help, and they were left for dead quickly by turn 5. Ut oh.

Drillers. Great win! They were patient and demonstrative, throwing down a 12-damage turn with two Chain Lightning's, a Lightning Bolt and a Fork. An earlier Ball Lightning and an Incinerate later, and this one was over. Very fun to play with this much firepower. Two Shivan's in-hand were insurance :)

Slivers. Bonnuit, Lining. That's the end of the season at the hands of a sweep. They had three mana in play but none produced the needed Forest to put a Muscle and a Victual Slivers in play. The early Crystalline was very helpful though.

NLFY. Redemption!!! No Landia wins this in a romp! The twin Black Vise's dealt 20 damage in 4 turns as the former champ struggled to put enough mana in play to start under the Vise's hand tax. Highly unusual for NLFY to win in this fashion but onto the next round!

Potatoes. They are stunned. The former champ brushed away the first loss in this series, grew a slight concern after the second loss and are downright speechless after this one. They were beaten to the punch by a notoriously slower team and will have an entire off season to think about it. Disappointing for the Potatoes.

Law. Saved by Pandemonium to win the game!! The Things had just played a Spirit of the Night & Nether Shadow and looked to be in control. Drawing the MVP enabled not one but two 'Nought's to enter the battlefield, doling out 24 damage in the process. HUGE win to propel the Law past the Things and into the next round!!!

Things. What happened...Well, their reliance on a 1-2 punch of Buried Alive into Living Death wasn't there in 3 of these games. The only time it showed up, Game 3, ended in victory. This team suddenly appears too vulnerable, and dependent, falling prey to a lowly foe like the Law. Need to look into complementary adjustments to allow for more defense.

Doom. The impossible made possible. Darwin skates on by thanks largely to a Sylvan Library, doling out muchos damage via a Ranger/Ants combo. A Tranquility saved them the game, as it destroyed two Megrim's in play with a certain Pox to follow. See ya, Fault!

Fault. May have been misplayed here, but the game was in-hand with the double-Megrim in play and a Pox to follow. Earlier Wheel of Fortune could have hurt them, though unclear.

Drillers. Two Mana Flare's give them 15 mana to work with. Unfortunately, they were low on direct damage spells. A Kaervek's Torch, Lightning Bolt, and an Incinerate later, and this game was over. 

Stiffs. Sitting on 9-mana with very little to summon is highly unusual. FS was hoping for a Meekstone and/or a Winter Orb to stop the madness, but never pulled either card.

Attritionists. Well that was awesome. Their full force sans Hecatomb came out on display in this one, as they totally wore down their opponent with Death Pits of Rath and Jinxed Idol to eliminate the creature base, Desolation to remove lands, Bottomless Pit for hand removal and finally, The Rack for some additional damage. They played only 2 creatures in this game. What fun.

Slivers. This is a tough team for them to beat, as shown in their last two meetings (0-2). With some plains/swamp help they may have changed their fate, but unlikely.

NLFY. Well, the hung in there, but never recovered after BAF beat them to the punch with L-Kill cards. They fell behind, and had no offense for the rest of the game. BAF killed off 8 total lands.

BAF. Jumped the L-Kill game due to turn-2 Sol Ring, played a Pox on turn-15 to shake things up a bit, and were able to survive Nevinyrral's Disk to put a Hidden Horror / Rukh combo in play for the win.

Potatoes. Were the constant aggressor in this one, but sealed their victory with a late-game Wrath of God. They had a bunch of creatures, help from a Crusade, and were able to defend against several creatures and enchantments from Darwin.

Darwin. Well, this is interesting. They withstood a quick attack from the Potatoes, then clearing the opposing attackers with a Theolnite Druid and Killer Bees in play to do the rest, but Wrath of God eliminated their chance to do combat. Tough loss for Darwin.

Things. An unconventional win here, taking full advantage of MVP Dark Ritual in their first turn to put a Hidden Horror in play, following it with a Fog Elemental two turns later. Easy win for the Things in five turns.

Law. Nothing here, again. The imbalance of their mana has struck yet another time. Might need to tweak the balance here some more.

Drillers. Long one, but win at the hands of two Balduvian Horde and 12 direct damage cards. They killed 9 opposing creatures in the process - reminiscent of their series against the Slivers. Great win.

Doom. Ut oh, not good. Pulling nine creatures with all of them ending up in the graveyard is pretty tough. Their only way to do damage is via creatures and if most of them are being destroyed immediately, then it's hard to see a path to victory. Could be the undoing of Darwin's run.

NLFY. In complete control the entire game. Several M-Kill cards put them in the driver seat early, and then a Icy helped immobilize the Law. A Sengir Vampire dealt most of the non-Black Vise damage, and was followed by a Demonic Hordes and another Sengir. No chance once the mana imbalance settled.

Law. Couldn't ever get anything going in this game. Fell victim to low mana base + Black Vise, and really didn't recover at all. This group needs all the stars to align in order for them to win, and they had nothing going in this one.

Law. Great win! They dodge a bullet by losing only one land in this game. Five other lands produce two Pandemonium cards and a game ending Nought. Seven other damage done by a Sandstalker and a lightning bolt.

NLFY. Not a game they could've won, I don't think. Early Black Vise helped them secure a lead but their usual offense-by-defense wasn't there.

Doom. Good win. The Sylvan Library helped big time and enabled much mana acceleration to put them out front. Damage was dealt by a pumped up Ants, a pumped up Theolite Druid and two Hurricane's (one killed a Shivan). Nice win and nicer control from the outset.

Drillers. Were stuck early on with low mana, but used six direct damage cards to take out opposing creatures. They had one Horde and one Shivan destroyed which was totally game-changing. Sat on two Furnace of Rath cards and didn't quite see a alley to play them. Same with Granite Gargoyle's.

NLFY. Grinding grinding grinding away. The all-blacks take down the Law with some clean defense and then ride a Demonic Hordes to victory. 

Law. Just didn't have enough lands in this - shocking. Only had 4 all game, and all were destroyed. They left game-winning combo in their hand stranded. Need more out of this team if they're going to contend!!!

Doom. Extremely well timed Hurricane gives the Doom the win. They lost six creatures in this game but held on with a Druid and a Wurm. Only four lands too.

Drillers. Felt behind this whole game despite having home court advantage. Losing a Horde and Shivan was critical.

NLFY. Great win. They looked behind from the initial draw, but were able to play sound defense thanks to the game MVP-Icy. They also dispatched 5 critical lands to freeze up the Law. A Sengir Vampire, Royal and Black Vise took care of the rest.

Law. Real tough loss. The winning combo was in the hand from the start but they couldn't get to 4-mana to summon a Pandemonium. They had a chance to level the game with a Wheel of Fortune but it didn't get them all the way there.

FD. Big comeback pushes this series back to even. They were down to 3 life, and had only dealt 6-damage themselves until two Shivan's and 7 lands put the game out of reach. Amassing Mountain's was critical in their victory to fuel the twin Dragon's.

Doom. So so close. They lost 5 creatures in this game due to direct damage and still had more offense to unleash. A Yavimaya Ants did serious damage, as did a Thelonite Druid ability. Two Djinn's in hand were somewhat useless against the double Dragon attack.

Drillers. Well well, old friend Furance of Rath sets the stage for 20 pts of damage in last turn of the game; 4 from Gargoyle's, then combined 16 from a Thunderbolt, Incinerate and Shock. Just in case, they were sitting on a Shivan with two more Mountains on the way. 

Doom. One more turn has them sending 20 damage via 5 Forest's courtesy of the Furnace. Didn't get there - game over.

NLFY. Two Black Vise's wreak havoc on the soon-to-be-former Murphy's Law. All damage was done by the twin Vise's except for a Choking Sands dealing two on a Plateau. NLFY won this series in a few different ways, which is always fun to see. They were able to win this game despite only having 3 Swamps in play.

Law. Combine mana issues with Black Vise's and you're toast. A dearth of defense completely disabled any chance they could win this game. Bigger issues despite a first round victory against fellow-combo-dependent; Things.

Doom. Huge victory thanks to big boy Force of Nature. Joined by a Yavimaya Ants, the Doom dealt eight damage in the final turn to win the game all because of trample. Nice. Earlier damage done by a Wurm, and an Archer.

Drillers. Had to use more direct damage than anticipated to kill off bigger creatures like the Force and the Fallow Wurm. No offense in this game.

Doom. Ah ha!!!!!!! There it is, a game 7 victory propelling the Qualifier Bracket champion to the Premier League finale. What a road! They won this thanks to Sylvan Library - picking off card by card to surge ahead - then a Force of Nature who just couldn't be taken down but the Drillers. A ton of mana helped tremendously. Great victory!

Drillers. What a disappointing way to go out - stuck on three mana to go down without much of a fight. They were dominated in this one, taking twenty damage via the Force and a Thelonite Druid. See ya, Drillers.

Murphy. Pandemonium enabled an early Phyrexian Dreadnought attack, then a 4th land on turn-7 let loose the Thundermare for the final ten damage. Glad to see some of their strengths come together finally.

NLFY. Sinkhole! Played on the second turn - how fun! Had Nevinyrral's Disk been played earlier, it would've been destroyed by Disenchant. Wasn't enough M-Kill early on to disable Murphys Momentum.

FD. Beneficiaries of two Mana Flare's that powered a 15-land Fireball to end the game. Power Surge made it onto the battlefield in this one as well, and was interesting. This might be more of a 'loss' for the Potatoes tho...

Potatoes. Sitting pretty with four creatures in play backfired, as they should've saved their last three mana for a Reverse Damage instead of putting a White Knight in play. Chalk this one up to 'user error'.

Darwin. Totally stifled by lack of mana + Dream Tides. The mana enabled them to summon 8 creatures, which is normally enough to take down a creature-less opponent, but the Dream Tides disabled their ability to untap at all. So, one attack per creature. Eeeeks. 

Icy Hot. HUGE win by the Hot! They rely entirely on Dream Tides in this one, stave off any potential issues (Force of Nature, Thelonite Druid) and take out Darwin with a Rathi Dragon. NIce!!

Slivers. Four slivers on the board, and all deposited into the graveyard by Living Death while well on their way to victory - the same old song.

Things. A Dark Ritual into Living Death has been devastating for opponents. In this one, the Things put a Polar Kraken (due to discard) and a Baron Sengir (due to Hidden Horror) into play for two turns of mayhem. Night night, Slivers.

Fault. Winning in 5-turns is almost unfair. This time, they used two M-Kill cards to stymie any momentum for the Stiffs, then capitalized on a Megrim w/ a Shocker and a Pox to win the game. 

Stiffs. What can you do in 4-turns? All 4 mana producers were killed, they took a hit on the Shocker to get a new hand, which backfired because they let go of a Tranquility, which could have saved them.

NLFY. What a game, coming down to a single point of life for the all-blacks. Two Terror's were absolutely critical to the win dispatching of an early Elder and a Fallow Wurm - both gave NLFY time to put some offense on the board in Sengir Vampire.

Doom. So close. They withstood more defense than maybe ever in their teams' history, losing 7 lands and 8 creatures. Just an absurd deficit to overcome. This is going to be a great series.

Doom. Skin of their teeth, the Doom take Game 2 thanks to a game-changing impact by City of Solitude. The City halted NLFYs ability to play defense with Icy Manipulator. A Force of Nature joined a Wurm, and they closed things out quickly.

NLFY. Drew a second swamp on turn-8, a tough draw for a roster of cards with BB casting costs. They destroyed only two lands and were left vulnerable for much of the game.

NLFY. Home team wins for the third time in a row this series. A first-turn Black Vise dealt 9 damage fast, then a second Vise helped end it two turns later. Two Sinkehole's put the hurt on and guaranteed victory.

Doom. No chance to get out in front of the M-Kill without an Elf or Elder.

Doom. Home field advantage helped them put a turn-2 Aluren in play, which also meant an Archers and a Wurm. The offense was on early for Darwin. They subsided on these two to take out NLFY a few turns later.

NLFY. Only one M-Kill card early, and it wasn't fast enough to disrupt Darwin's speed. They also didn't have enough C-Kill - one Hellfire but it was too late to summon.

NLFY. Wow, they were down to 1pt after the 7th turn. Then, the MVP Royal started to do work against a strong Doom creature offensive, killing 3 in the process as they attacked relentlessly. A Black Vise helped early on, and a Terror was key to dispatch a Djinn too. Huge comeback win.

Doom. Well that might be the series - had the home team down big and just couldn't finish the job. One more turn gets them the W, but it wasn't there.

Doom. Good rebuttal by the Doom. In their 15 drawn cards, they had 8 lands -- huge against a M-Kill team like NLFY. Not only that, but their seven other cards included two Giant Growth's for an Archer, an Ants and a game-ending Hurricane. Niceeeeee.

NLFY. No chance in this one. Darwin simply outdrew them. Mana struggles didn't help either.

NLFY. In what looked to be No Landia's undoing, they drew two Black Vise's early and were able to subside on only one land for the first four turns. They used a Dark Ritual to destroy an Forest and killed an Elvish Archers with a Terror. Annnnnnnd that's it! Fastest game possible for this team in a Game 7 clincher!!

Doom. Disappointing way to go out - mana strapped against the most devastating M-Kill team in the league. No one wins this game against NLFY, but an admirable run by the Qualifier Bracket winner.

Sliver. This is the Sliver Team we're used to - 6 different slivers on the board at one time, with a finishing move of Coat of Arms. Yow!

Darwin. Three lands with only two creatures is a recipe for disaster against the slivers.

NLFY. WHAT A GAME!!!! A total of 15 turns to decide this in favor of No Landia. Would LOVE to go into the details, but my better half is demanding I get ready for dinner. Outrageous, I know. Anyway, they rely on killing off 8 mana, a game-saving Icy Manipulator to stall a Spirit of the Night for a ridiculous comeback victory. Awesome game.

Things. Had this in the bag with their near-unstoppable Spirit of the Night until NFLY drew the only card that could have saved them. Tough loss.

Law. HELL YEAH! The architecture finally came to fruition in this one in a wild finish against the defending-champ. Four turns in, the Law had a Pandemonium on the board and little else. The Potatoes played an Armageddon thinking it'd be the only way to stop whatever madness was to come. In three successive turns, the Law drew a Plains, a Sol Ring and a Mountain. Those four mana gave way to a Lava Hounds, another Pandemonium, a Angelic Renewal to set them up for another creature. On their 9th turn, they drew the monster of all monsters in Phyrexian Dreadnought and played him for 48 total damage (2 Pandemonium + Angelic Renewal) - the most damage ever done in a single turn and knocking the Potatoes back to a historic -40 poitns. Ay carumba, what a win.

Potatoes. A Tundra Wolves, Wild Aesthir and a Sol Ring in played meant their Armageddon on turn-4 made perfect sense. They didn't plan on the bounce-back from Murphys Law, or going land-less in their next 5 turns. Tough loss.

Stiffs. Fascinating game. A Winter Orb made it out early, stifling The Attritionists' chance at maximizing Hecatomb. Sealing the deal, though, was actually newcomer Lake of the Dead, who popped onto the battlefield, took advantage of it's abilities and plopped a Yawgmoth Demon right into play. Two turns later, this one was over. 

Attritionists. Likely win this game without Winter Orb on the board. Had strong mana (6), a Hecatomb and Death Pits of Rath in their hand for extra coverage.

Potatoes. Beats NLFY by Armageddon - a trend with these two. Still, No Landia's response to White Knight with Icy Manipulator was helpful, along with a Royal to threatened destruction for any other creature. A Crusade put their two Soul Warden's, Knight and Tundra Wolves over the top for the win.

NLFY. They're getting there. This loss marked progress against a team that has absolutely had their number in recent match-ups. Feels like some additional creatures would be helpful, and I think I know just the thing..

Icy. Wins this one on the heels of two Pyrokinesis cards. The first destroyed a Hidden Horror, and the second won the game. Playing Mana Barbs early totally changed the way the game was played and, really, Icy Hot could just sit and wait for the Pyro to be drawn while the Things hurt themselves turn after turn.

Things. Couldn't draw the necessary 2MC Grave-Res cards to put a monster onto the battlefield. Instead, they continually hurt themselves by Mana Flare. Showed the need for some H-Pump in the form of a Demonic Tutor..

Drillers. Good win for the Drillers here. They destroyed four opposing creatures early, then leveraged a MVP Mana Flare to fuel a Shivan, Gargoyle, and Fireball's to seal the deal. A Power Surge was helpful too.

Attritionists. Landed most of their foundation, and even saw three Sengir Autocrat's hit the battlefield. Unfortunately, there was no Hecatomb in sight, so they were torn to shreds with the superior offense from FD. A Desolation helped dim some of the pain, but they couldn't last.
Somewhat uneventful game where Darwin raced out with offense faster than the Frozen Stiffs could summon defense. A game Darwin needed to win, and did.

Slivers. Pump out 7 creatures quickly and win this game in their next turn if not for Final Fortune.

Murphy's Law. ONE plains spells a world of hurt for the Slivers here. A Pandemonium, a Dreadnought, two Enlightened Tutor's...one more plains.

Drillers. Starting to think they'll never beat a team like the Things. There's just no way for them to deal with the sheer heft of the Things' creature base. The best they can do is hope for mana struggles or a faster start to the game. 

Things. Use a Dark Ritual to summon Living Death and bring a Polar Kraken into play on the fifth turn. Two plays later, and a Fog Elemental and Nether Shadow joined in on the fun to -11.

Icy Hot. Another victory for the Hot! This time, they ride an early Rathi Dragon to deal the first ten damage, then sit on some direct damage to do some more, then a second Manabarbs (first one was destroyed) to finish them off. Worth noting that Maze of Ith was extremely helpful in this game, keeping any single creature attack from the Stiffs completely at bay.

Stiffs. Interesting game for them. They froze the board early with a Winter Orb and Meekstone, then struggled to put any offense in play. A Titania's Song could've enlivened five artifacts, but it would've freed the Rathi in the process. One C-Kill could've changed that, but it never came.

Attritionists. Interesting win against their fellow bottom-feeder. They use a first turn Bottomless Pit to rid NLFY of their hand, including a Sengir Vampire, Black Knight and Demonic Horde. It did the same to their own hand, but it didn't matter at all. They could sit by waiting for The Rack and Jinxed Idol to clean up the mess. Great win by them, and makes me think Bottomless Pit should be more of a factor.

NLFY. Tough loss here. They drew more than half their total lands in this game, but only 3 creatures with offense. They could have summoned ANYTHING, and were eventually done-in by the Jinxed Idol. Too bad, I was excited to see them win one with their new FAs.

Darwin. Wow, nice 5-turn win by the Doom. They leveraged Fyndhorn Elder for some serious speed here, who helped put out an Elvish Archers, a Thelonite Druid and Y-Ants. That alone led to 10-damage in one turn, then another 11-damage the next to finish off the Law. Nice.

Murphy's Law. Two turns away from victory, they sat with three lands on the board, two more coming up and a hand full'a mayhem. Tough loss.

Potatoes. Ride a Crusade, a Icatian Town and a Kjeldoran Outpost to victory. Land Tax helped also. Little resistance from the Fault in this one. 

Fault. Not nearly enough defense to withstand the quick attack from the Potatoes.

Potatoes. Barely scraped by for the win thanks to a game-saving Wrath of God. They had four creatures on the battlefield, two of them frozen, and were facing three bruisers when the WoG came. It enabled the Potatoes a free path to victory two turns later with a Icatian Town. Two Crusades, a Land Tax, a Zuran Orb were all instrumental in this victory. Not sure they could've prevailed without those cards.

Stiffs. One card away from winning. If not for the WoG, they attack with a 7/7 Entropic Specter, a Yawgmoth Demon and a Llanowar Elf for the win. Great to see their core dynamics in play with Meekstone, Winter Orb, Instill Energy all contributing to the effort in a nice way. Great promise for this team.

Drillers. Nice, a new strategy coming together in this one with Urzatron + Mana Flare to do 20-damage in one turn. A Fireball for 10, a Bolt for 3, and a Shivan for the remaining 7. So fun to see.

Slivers. Had the early makings of their usual fast attack with a Metallic, Muscle and Clot Sliver, but a Fireball knocked two of them out. Ton of mana, nothing else to summon.

Fault. Maybe the best win for them all season. They were under heavy fire from Darwin, dropping most of their life total as they slowly ground away Darwin's mana supply. An early discard helped put a Rukh onto the battlefield, but it was Megrim who strung them to victory.
