Fault. Put the pressure on early with a Shocker dealing 13-damage in one turn thanks to a Dark Ritual into Megrim. Were kept at bay despite doubling Megrim and Shocker, but won it with a Hyppy who couldn't be blocked.

Things. Didn't have much of any offense this game. Deserved to lose.

Stormy. Perfect antidote to Extinction, destroying three of four creatures with DD. Two Ball Lightning, an Earthquake, an Incinerate and a Fireball did the rest. I like Earthquake on this team.

Extinction. Still missing something on this team and I'm not sure what it is - maybe even more creatures (Mtenda Lions). Lotta lands with nothing to summon against a creatureless team.

Slivers. No contest after killing Su-Chi.

11. One swamp killed them.

NLFY. No contest.

Hot. Stink.

Potatoes. Lose this game, fast, without two Plowshares who took care of back-to-back Noughts. A trio of Tundra Wolves took care of the rest - in part due to a Crusade. Armageddon always kept Monsters from countering anything.

Monsters. Tough loss. Had all the makings of a beat down but the Potatoes had the only two cards that could have saved them.

Gloves. A WILD finish! The game was over, the final count we reverting taken until an error had been detected. Teferi's Puzzle Box occurs 2x when there are two in play - a big learning. As a result, a desperation move by the Gloves to use a Wheel of Fortune ends up winning the game by drawing a Dark Ritual and a Puzzle Box. They go into play to join another Box, a Howling Mine and a U-Dreams. The Wheel caused 7-damage, then the two Box and Mine caused another 13-damage. That's 20-damage in one turn!!!! This is precisely why this deck was built 👍

Darwin. Have the victory ripped out from under them in devastating fashion. What a finish.

Things. Nothing like grabbing a W by casting only two cards. Living Death is just deadly.

Stormy. Needed a bit more offense to win this one.

Slivers. No contest. A Queen sighting though!!!

Hot. Stink.

NLFY. Redemption can be a sweet song. They destroy four mana producers to totally disable Team 11 and ride two Vise to victory. This is a very good, simple team.

Team 11. Couldn't get off the ground after losing three lands and one Elves. Tough loss.

Potatoes. Hung around, knocked out two creatures with Balance, then used an Outpost and duo of Factory to win the game.

Doom. Their game to lose. Sat on two Library, giving them 5 cards to reveal per turn and were stuck with five lands at the top of their library.

Gloves. Train is rolling - four in a row now! Serious manipulation of the Monsters here as they flipped the hands more than a few times with Winds of Change. One of them was used after the Monsters pulled a Mask into their hand with a Nought in the way. A U-Dreams ground them away down to rubble.

Monsters. The Field of Dreams-Millstone combo is nice and helps protect them as intended. Still taking a while to find the deadly combo but it still looks okay.

Fault. An incredible comeback. They were down 20 to 2 and crawled all the way back to victory. They destroyed 6 total lands, used a Tutor to pull a game saving Megrim, and put a Pox in play that shifted the balance. Great win.

Stormy. Threw 7DD spells at them and came up short. Losing lands was critical as two Ball Lightning we're stranded time and time again. Came down to the wire.

Extinction. Great win! A timely Giant Growth helps a Lhurgoyf extinguish a massively pumped Nether Shadow to keep their hopes alive and eventually close out. Another Goyf came, as did another Ape. Great win.

Things. Ridiculous string of Unstable Mutation to pump up the Shadow 3x. Otherwise, though, they didn't have much with three Buried Alive in their hand which would have pumped the opposing Goyf.

NLFY. Cruise to ready victory after destroying two of 11s three lands.

11. Had nothing in this one.

Hot. Wow, just when they had been promised dead! The Hot pull a fast one on the league's top team by sitting on a Power Surge all the way to victory! Wall of Great was critical in stopping there Sliver attack, and they just sat there.

Slivers. Pull three creatures and half their mana against the one team that encourages you to use mana. What a snafu!

Doom. Wow, crawl out with a victory thanks to two Instant spells on their final turn; Crumble and Giant Growth. The later helped pump a Elves earlier in the game as well, as Darwin ends a 4-game winning streak for the Gloves.

Gloves. Dang, their early Winds of Change was huge, giving them two more U-Dreams, a Vise and two Shocker. Ran out of time thanks to the Y-Ants. Their own Howling Mine enabling there Giant Growth that won Darwin the game. Might need some C-Kill in this deck.

Things. Great revenge game for the underdog Things. They put 20-points of damage on in two turns thanks to a Horror and Kraken.

Fault. Once the big guy hit the graveyard on turn-3, it wasn't looking good. They had no C-Kill, and could only hope to discard key cards.

Extinction. Nice win, use two Lion, an Ape and a Djinn to take it home. Big Worldly Tutor to bring out the Djinn.

Stormy. Tough game, couldn't do much but wait for not mountains.

Sliver. Acidic Sliver wins the game for the Slivers who remove a Metallic Sliver to kill off a Assassin.

11. Didn't have much here, may not be built for the fast opponent.

Hot. Second win in a row!! Two Control Magic win them two Vampire, and the dual Tower pumped then to record high in life. Can't believe they beat two top teams in a row.

NLFY. Shocked! Had an early Black Knight killed, then both Vampire stolen. No other offense despite killing off most of opposing mana supply.

Potatoes. No contest. Two White Knight completely dismantle the Gloves.

Gloves. No defense against COP Black creatures. Hmmmm.

Monsters. Three Maze of Ith save the day! Deadly Nought makes it it on 7th turn, aided by a Phantasmal Mount, and wins the game on the 8th turn.

Doom. Locked and loaded in this one but came up short. Eight creatures hit the battlefield but we're stymied by the Maze

Extinction. Might have been the best game of the season. Their game plan really came into effect here, outlasting there land destruction from BAF with three Birds, then subsiding on Chaosphere and two Angelic Renewal to take down a Rukh, then power Mirri to do here thing. A late Lhurgoyf joined the fray as well at 5/6. A first turn Worldly Tutor was big to bring in a Birds. They lost six lands and still came from behind to win.

Fault. A little more offense and they have this wrapped up. Summoned only three creatures, and all there were killed off. The Megrim in play did nothing which was odd given how much mana was destroyed - X was just that fast.

Stormy. Win a big one thanks to a board clearing Earthquake, Mana struggles for the Things and a Horde. Balance shifted a few times.

Things. Needed this one to gain some ground in the middle of the pack.

Sliver. Boom baby! Dispatch NLFY as fast as they arrived, using a City of Brass to power five slivers en route to s shellacking. The Heart Sliver and Muscle Sliver played big parts.

Landia. Drew a Mulligan to start and never recovered. Couldn't kill the City of Brass and were too far behind to defend the Sliver attack.

Hot. Incredible!! A third win in a row!!!! Stealthy play using bounce cards and counters to keep Team 11 from completing an attack were huge. Propaganda was key paired with a Manabarbs, and that was that.

11. Tough loss. Too a lot of damage to get attackers in play and couldn't keep them there. Frustrating loss.

Darwin. Big win! Showed a nice blend of offense and defense in this game, dispatching two opposing creatures while attacking with a Djinn and a Force.

Potatoes. Imbalance of mana and three Land Tax left then weak. Tough loss.

Gloves. Outrageously fun game. The Gloves put the heat on with two Vise, two Mine,a Puzzle Box and a Underworld Dreams. Dealt seven critical damage in the last turn to win.

Monsters. I love this team. They're so close to being a tough opponent. Still have to work hard for their Ws but that's alright. Had a hidden Nought on the board but didn't have time to get him out.

Team 11. Drew well with two Vise in hand to immediately put the Things into single-digits. One more Vise and two Su-Chi later and this one was over. Came down to a critical last draw for the Things.

Things. If they had drawn a mana on the last play, they win. They didn't, so down they went! Two Buried Alive put a bevy of stoutly blokes in the graveyard ready for resuscitation.

Potatoes. Win a big game to stave off the encroaching 3 seed. Land destruction was key - Armageddon killed off five mana to turn the game. Zuran Orb wins MVP for granting an additional 14 life to keep them alive.

Stormy. Use all four Ball Lightning and several DD spells but just couldn't rebound after the Potatoes drew a second Orb.

NLFY. Wins easily thanks to an Icy. It disabled Fault and paved a path to victory behind 2 Royal Assassin, a Sengir and a Demonic Hordes. Easy win once the locks were in place.

Fault. Stumbled out the gate. Didn't have enough offense, defense or anything to keep them in this.

Monsters. Drew very well - Mask, Nought, Barrier, Meekstone. They were out in front almost without laying down a card. The mix is working, I think. I like the synergies of throwing Ornithopter in there as well. Not sure about Phantasmal Mount but it's ok for now.

Hot. Had nothing going, though they did steal the Nought!

Extinction. BEAUTIFUL win. They drew two lands all game - TWO - and carefully, meticulously pulled Doom apart. Outstanding win. A key difference maker was new FA Kudzu! Destroying two lands! Two BoPs and a Sol Ring kept X afloat with a Lhurgoyf that pushed them to victory. Great win. Dispatched 4 opposing creatures, too. Had two WoGs in hand also. Awesome win.

Doom. Lost the upper hand once Kudzu entered the field. They sat on a Ants, a Ranger and some more defense.

Gloves. Keep this train rolling!!! The Gloves take out the #1-seed in style with a Vise, two U-Dreams, a Howling Mine with an Entropic Specter and a Wil-o on the frontline. Great win.

Slivers. Tough loss, had no E-Kill and it cost them. Did put a Sliver Queenie in play which, of course, is always fun.

Doom. Drew exceptionally well to thwart 10s impending attack. Two defensive cards - Tranquility & Verdigris - we're critical in paving the way for a Force of Nature.

10. Had 11 mana on their second turn thanks to three Mana Vault's. Crazy. Had Planar Gate destroyed early which really hurt them. A late Yawggy was there but no match for the big boy in green. Royal Assassin was left stranded in hand too. Juuuuust about there!

Potatoes. Win a battle of attrition against their rival. Used a Balance to knock out opposing lands and a Sengir Vampire. FAs like Land Tax and Cleansing really hamper NLFY. They could rely on cheaper creatures much more so than NLFY could, and win as a result.

NLFY. Amazing they were even able to dole out 11-damage in this one. They have real problems with the Potatoes new emphasis on land destruction, life pump and mana pump. Balance may have shifted toward the Potatoes in this match-up of Original 10 teams.

Monsters. Nice win. They drew well with a Mask-Nought in the initial hand, then had protection with multiple counters as they played chicken with 3 disguised Ornithopter. Two Grave-Res were key to retrieve the fallen Nought mid-game.

Things. Were quick and nimble with two Shadow and two Devils but didn't have any defense for the Nought.

Hot. Sat back and waited - just like at the end of S5. Propaganda and Mana Leak helped hugely, and Maze of Ith was valuable too. Rathi came in late to clean up the rest. 

Glop. Lotta wheel spinning for them. Ton of mana, but no offense. They were sitting and waiting too but outside of a Storm Seeker they had no way to deal damage. We'll see if that continues to be a problem.

Stormy. Cards like Earthquake and Pyroclasm are total game-changers against the Slivers. They used both in this game and extinguished three opponents. Slivers put out a Furnace of Rath that would have ended the game a turn later. Stormy had two draws left before going down to a Muscle Sliver - the third of the game - and pulled a Ball Lightning. Nice.

Slivers. Lost by the skin of their teeth. Lost 6 of 7 creatures in this game in a total card draw-down. One of the best games of the year.

Gloves. WHOA NELLY! Great win by the Gloves! Their synergy was off the charts, playing defense with two pesky Wisp to keep two Shocker at bay while two Howling Mine, two U-World Dreams and a Black Vise closed things out. They won this game on the last draw of the game, putting a second Mine, Dreams and the Vise into play which dealt 7 damage to put the Fault away. Great game.

Fault. So close, but what a match-up. These two teams are exact opposites and it's OUTSTANDING to pit them against one another. Had this wrapped up with one turn remaining. Lost a key Mind Twist earlier in the game, too.

Fault. Nice win, just eroding the Potatoes hand with the Hyppy. They didn't have much else but were able to destroy a few lands that happy them in the clear.

Potatoes. Rough game without mana. Puts them into a battle for the #1 seed with the Slivers in the final week of the season.

Team 11. Incredible win - a Su-Chi flanked by two Elves and a Yawgmoth Priest took care of the near defenseless Gloves. A Winter Orb down the stretch proved pivotal, as it froze out the opponent from a playing a game-ending Sudden Impact.

Gloves. SO CLOSE! The ridiculous M-Pump of Team 11 (3x Mana Matrix, two Elves, Priest) disabled three Black Vise despite sitting on a Howling Mine - really incredible loss vs. it being an incredible win for the 11.

NLFY. Demonic Hordes were critical in disabling the Monsters. The game also heavily changed when Hellfire scratched two Noughts from the field.

Monsters. Roster is solid but it's missing something. Not much creature defense available.

Doom. Nonchalant win facing very little pressure outside of initial STDevils. 

Things. Drew more lands than spells cast.

What's At Stake -- If the Slivers win, they'll have pulled 0.5 games behind the Potatoes with one game left to go. If they beat BAF in the final game, they have a shot at the regular season title. 

If the Potatoes win, the #1-seed is theirs!

Gloves. So fun!! The synergy on this team was awesome. They played one creature all game - Shocker - and he recorded not one point of damage. Black Vise took the first load, then a second Underworld Dreams paired exceptionally well with a Winds of Change and Teferi's Puzzle Box to knock out the Artists. What a fun team.

Artists. Had a tough time getting going with all the hand shuffling. Tough opponent for them because their mana manipulation didn't help much.

What's at Stake:

Doom. If they can win here, they increase their chances of bypassing the first round of the Qualifier Playoff AND to possibly grab the #7-seed vs. #8.

Hot. Nothing, they're terrible.

Stormy. Patience paid off here. They held as many DD spells as possible for the Mask'ed Noughts and when they came, they were gone just as fast. A Meekstone slowed down their creature-led assault a bit, but it was only a matter of time.

Monsters. Looked VERY one dimensional in this game. May need to drop the updated rules for Mask-Nought to give them more heft. It's proving difficult a combo to build around as we near another down season with these two at the core.

Things. Turn 1 Buried Alive. Turn 3 Living Death. Turn 4 twenty-eight damage. Turn 5 eighteen damage. SEEEEEE YA Hot.

Hot. Welp, that was brutal.

Sliver Lining. BOOOOOOM BABY! That's a smackdown right there. The Fault draw nothing of interest and the Slivers slap them back into concrete.

Fault. LIMPING into the playoffs after this dismantling. Almost need the Qualifier to find some momentum, but they don't have the luxury...

NLFY. Ugly game won entirely by a Black Vise. Literally nothing else happened. These guys are cruising for the playoffs.

Stormy. Mana shortage hurt them for the L.

X. Sat tight on a Savannah Lion and THREE Giant Growth for the win. Pushes their win streak to 7 in a row to head into the playoffs. Awesome turnaround.

Team 11. Needed a bit more mana to put some ammo out there.

7.commentary:
FD. Played a nice game in this one led, ironically, by Power Surge! Who, I might, add was reintroduced to the roster for the playoffs. It was a balanced attack in this game of both creature attack and direct damage. Looked like a real team here..

TF. Had the makings of a Hecatomb but didn't quite get there. Same with their big guy, which maybe poses an issue on hierarchy here.

Things. Win #1 in the books! They handle the new Hotness thanks to FA signing Flying Men + Unstable Mutation. Another pumped creature - Nether Shadow - helped, then a "Certain Death" combo ended things on turn-7. 

Hot. I like the look despite the L. A Mudslide slowed the Things down. A Fellwar Stone & Sol Ring helped pumped the mana department, and two Dark Maze sat on the battlefield playing. Two Drain Power in hand just in case the Manabarbs showed up.

Potatoes. Picked up right where they left off. The Potatoes looked mean in this game, arming themselves with a Wolves and Knight and two Crusade that benefited from a turn-5 Armageddon to knock out the seemingly slow Doom.

Doom. Funny, they seem SLOW these days!!! Wonder how to speed them up and put more pump into them. There may be too many good teams focused on dismantling their mana supply to stay as is.

Gloves. Really nice win, load up on 3x Howling Mine, put a Underworld Dreams out there and with one Winds of Change it was over.

Boom. Looked terrifically slow in this game.

Slivers. Knock off the Fault in six quick turns. No contest here, they rode Crystalline, Heart, Muscle, Metallic, Winged Slivers to victory behind a City of Brass.

Fault. Short on mana left them short on, well, anything.

FD. Smoke show! They lit up the Thrulls in an absolute firestorm and advance to the next round!! Some last minute roster changes really paving the way, as Power Surge and Balduvian Horde are paying big dividends. Nice decisive series win over a truly troubled opponent.

TF. Well, better luck next year? Once again, they had the makings - multiple creatures + Hecatomb - but couldn't make it happen. Similar to the Big Gloves and U-Dreams, the Hecatomb strategy may have to be secondary. Can a "black weenie" deck work? We have to find out.

Gloves. Looking good there, Gloves! Three big turns in this no contest; 

T2 - Draw/Play Sulfurous Springs, Dark Ritual, U-Dreams & Shocker in play
T3 - 15x Damage w/ Shocker and Winds of Change
T6 - 2x Winds of Change deal 10 Damage for the win

Blue looked reasonable in this game, with the LoT almost making it out onto the field of battle!

Thrulls. Looked pretty rough. Absolutely no defense for the Gloves.

Boom. Dragged this thing out and got the W. Not a fast squad and didn't play it that way. They drew enough mana to thwart the Entropic Specter, leveraged Titania's Song and took out home.

Gloves. Had an early lead but found their offense stunted as soon as the Specter ran out of gas and their U-Dreams was countered.

Birds. Start undefeated!!! No contest win here, though they did put out most of their game plan; 4x Falcon, Kismet, Meekstone, Castle. Cool to see.

Facts. Yeeeeesh, started with a predominantly green hand and only summoned one Forest all game. No offense or defense came, but it's only one game :)

Drillers. Oh man, I love this Team. Any game that features a Shivan, Crimson Hellkite, Rock Hydra and a Fire Drake is a beauty. That's precisely what happened this game with an early Mana Flare. Fire rained down on their mono-red nemesis.

Dark n Stormy. Well, that was embarrassing. The Drillers stomped out their flame with mana multipliers as promised. DnS drew one Mountain all game and sat idly by get lit the F up. Sort of hilarious.

Gloves. Win a thriller! They were down early, mid game but not when it counted! Draw 2x U-Dreams before Titania's Song made it's way out - a card that really stifles this Gloves flow. Just as they're withstanding attack after attack, they draw a third U-Dreams organically to put the nail in the coffin for Peak-a-Boom. Had a feeling they'd win this series, love to fight another day in the Qualifier bracket!

Boom. Lackluster finish, I must admit. They're slow and don't control games well enough to produce wins. It might require a different primary strategy than the Wurm combo...

NLFY. Good win. They were in control from the get-go, using a Strip Mine to destroy a Savannah. They destroyed three other lands to control Xs mana supply, then used a N-Disk when X was starting to gain momentum and finished 'em off with a Black Knight and Sengir. Love the revised Black Knight and use of Sengir's in this deck.

Extinction. Right there despite no mana. They put several BoPs out, a Sol Ring, and were still somehow in it. Had two Balduvian Horde in hand - fun to see them involved.

Team 10. Look at these guys!! Surplus of mana put a Vise-Ivory Tower out in play, then two Mishra's Workshop went on the offensive. A W-Treefolk followed and turned up the heat. No contest. 

Monsters. Flat start :( Decent mana, but failed to show any O or D.

Gloves. It's a game like this that makes me think the Gloves are too good to dismantle. Turn-4 shows Megrim, then a Swamp-Dark Ritual-Underworld Dreams. Both sides coming to party. The next turn see a Shocker for the Fault and the Gloves get wild; Wheel of Fortune + Shocker of their own, then Winds of Change for 13x damage. One more Shocker, and yet another WoC out y the Fault in the hole another 11x for the upset win. Pretty sick.

Fault. Drew a great hand, and list it almost immediately. They aren't out of this yet!

FD. Win a make-up game despite my advancing fatigue!!! Power Surge would've won MVP in either contest - don't know why I banished this card in favor of Mana Flare!! That said, they combined to pummel YCBI in Game 1. These teams are well-balanced - the initial game going 26 turns! FD has slight edge in this series pretty much because of Power Surge.

YCBI. They have their work cut out for them in this match-up. They can handle the creature attack that FD comes in with, and are armed with some only some counters for DD + Power Surge. Victory is probably Serra Angel / Archangel attacking a flat defense.

Gloves. First turn Dark Ritual into U-Dreams. Second turn mana into Sol Ring. Third turn nothing, then fourth turn Puzzle Box. Fifth turn Puzzle Box. This game was over on the fifth turn with 13x damage done via 2x Box.

Fault. Looking really uninspired here, but this was as fast a victory as we're likely to see all season.

FD. Power Surge stiles again, dealing as much as 8x damage on a single turn. They're not winning this series with creatures or DD, it seems, which is surprising to me. Two games in now, less surprising.

YCBI. Amazingly, this team is doing a fantastic job hanging opposing creatures. They do, however, need to rethink their strategy when it comes to mana and be wary of the now two-time game MVP in Power Surge.

Sliver. No contest, send out 6 Slivers and go to work against defenseless Hot.

Hot. No creatures, only defense was a Mana Leak and Propaganda, and the latter was nullified but Crystalline Sliver.

Gloves. Bring out the brooms!!!! it's a second round sweep! The Gloves win the unconventional way - by sheer force of Entropic Specter pummeling BAF several times to get the W. Talk about an instant impact FA signing!!! They didn't face much opposition thanks to the Fault's mana glitch early on. Somehow, some way, the #15 seeded Big Gloves make it into the Premier Bracket.

Fault. They lost by 14, 24 and 25. This was a really, really ugly showing by the Fault. They were slow, disjointed and unable to play any D agains the Gloves. I wonder if this was just a bad match-up for them because I do think this is a good team.

YCBI. Use a Castle to play major D against the DD spells of the Drillers. Used a WoG, too, to knock out a growing Rock Hydra, which was key.

FD. Castle is a problem - it makes killing a measly 3/3 so mana-intensive. FD knows they have to preserve their Fireball and Rolling Thunder spells for the bigger dogs, not the Marble TItan's of the world. Maybe they could use a few Lightning Bolt...

YCBI. Don't call it a comeback!!! The Drillers were mana strapped and the Birds took advantage. Their Sol Ring enables 2x Archangel to hit the battlefield and lay waste to the Drillers. Almost a no contest once they were out.

FD. Hit some mana issues and couldn't recover. Sat on a Shatter the ENTIRE GAME instead of using it on the game-MVP Sol Ring :(

DnS. Win the usual way but it took 11 turns. Cluster of with two Ball Lightning paved the way. Love Smoke addition. Two-headed big guy was lying in wait. Lot of fun.

Fax. Will be one of the primary focus areas this season. There's something here, something fun and great and different, but I don't know what it is yet.

YCBI. Damnnnn!!!! On a roll now! They defeat the Drillers by winning their third in a row to take the series. Dad would be proud!!! Once again, the Castle + flying creatures was too much for FD. Archangel wreaks havoc once more (how about that FA signing!), and on they go to face Darwin in Round 3.

FD. Damnnn, what a disappointment. They drop three in a row to lose the series in what started to feel like an insurmountable foe. Coincidentally, Power Surge - the MVP in Game 1 & 2 - did not play a role in Games 3-5. It showed up once and was quickly disenchanted. Solid surge here by FD to finish the season on an exciting note, one of hope. Maybe there's something to be done with this squad after all.

MM. Nice win!! Three counters dispatched a WoG, Lhurgoyf and something else. A Mask enabled a Nought,a Copy Nought, then a Reconstruction brought the big guy back and a Maze of Ith brought him to life. A Juggernaut joined the fray and a Invoke Prejudice played D. Lotta fun to see these cards come together.

X. Tough loss, lose 5 creatures and jumped out to a quick 20-11 lead but ran out of defense despite casting two WoG.

Team 10. Impressive win! They played 9 cards in 9 turns, which has not been a pace Yawggy Teams of old could really pull off. They had a Birds, two Fellwar Stone and ample mana to play offense resulting in four creatures summoned; STDevil, Archers, Jugg and a Treefolk that was killed. Nice win, and even more promising victory.

NLFY. Drew two lands and stood pat.

Doom. Take advantage of a mana/color imbalance by YCBI with a quick win.

YCBI. White hand, islands only.

NLFY. Get a nice win here. Use 3x Sinkhole, a Demonic Horde and Cyclopean Tomb to nullify 6x Mountain. The Horde was easily able to chip 20 off thanks to a Hellfire that took out a Wall of Earth and a Granite Gargoyle. Looked strong in this game.

DnS. Had struck an early lead but once the Tomb hit the board it was a race to keep pumping out mountains.

Things. Necromancy saves them from the abyss that is the Thrulldrums! Built a lead on a Mutation-Flying Men and Fog Elemental, then put a SotN into play for the win.

Thrulls. Used a Blood Pet one turn short of putting a Hecatomb in play. No creature was drawn for the rest of the game of course.

Potatoes. A really nice win. They handled a sturdy opponent surgically. More creatures and more defense, specifically turn 8 when a Wolves was summoned in addition to a Balance while also activating Zuran Orb for 9 life and a decimation of opposing resources.

Fault. Had something brewing but couldn't contend with the Potatoes balance.

Things. Ho-lee smackdown! The Things enter the playoffs with a few new cards up their sleeves. The introduction of red now feels like it was a long time coming, enabling the Things to pick and choose which burly brutes best foil the opponent. This time, an early Buried Alive gave way to El Capitano and 2x Crimson Hellkite en route to an absolute barrage of hellflames the next turn. Suddenly, the Things look unstoppable.

Team 10. Ut oh...They definitely dont have enough defense to contend with the Things' front court. Their only hope in this series is to be super fast, put Winter Orb out every game and hope the Things stumble in the mana department..

Gloves. By the skin of their teeth. This went down to the wire, with the Gloves only hope in pulling a game-saving U-Dreams. What they do on turn-13? Pulled a U-Dreams!! Wild win.

Hot. Kicking themselves after this loss. They did it to themselves, getting cute in the final rounds with a third Manabarbs to strap the Gloves to one last summon. Foolish move.

Doom. Great comeback win. They were down 18-4 before whipping out the Hurricane for five then 17 more the next trip attacks.

Sliver. Had this wrapped up until Hurricane got them!

YCBI. Much more their style; lots of unlockable winged offense and lots of defense. Two Pacifism were key, as was a timely WoG. Nice win.

Doom. Real close loss. Mid-game Hurricane was big, and summoned six creatures to put the pressure on, but they were out drawn.

NLFY. Surgical win playing into the dynamism and strength of their roster. Two Vise were on the board early to put pressure on Birds-Y, but on the 7th turn this happened...
- Sac Swamp for Lake of the Dead, Sol Ring, Demonic Tutor for Hellfire to kill all four opposing creatures, opening up the battlefield for an untouched Black Knight to deal the remaining two damage of the game. Outstanding.

YCBI. Right there against one of the All-Time Greats. Two Bay Faclon, Soraya and a Wall of Swords in play but were getting crushed by the dual-Vise.

DnS. Great response in Game 2! Trot out 6x creatures to wear down NLFY to even the series. Used both Shatter early on a Black Vise & Nevinyrral's Disk which dramatically changed the landscape of the game.A Pyroclasm + Fork was huge as well, dispatching 2x Sengir Vampire and a Wall of Spears. Ultimately, more creatures won.

NLFY. No land destruction in sight! A game after crushing 6x of them, how strange!? More to come in Game 3!

Things. This is what's different about this team...on 3x mana, they're able to engineer a victory thanks to H-Pump; Demonic Tutor. Just like last game, they leveraged a different strategy - Draw-Advantage - to power the win. Pretty cool.

Team 10. Pretty clear there's no way for them to stop the Spirit of the Night. I don't really see how they'll win this series unless they can use Winter Orb to stifle the Things.

Monsters. THIS is PRECISELY why I built this team. The synergy throughout was rhythmic; Field of Dreams and Sindbad provided a draw engine, Ray of Erasure with F-Dreams was magical for defense, and they could sit back and wait for the Mask-Nought combo. A Transmute Artifact made a showing and the Monsters outlasted the very different-feeling Stormy Team.

DnS. Doesn't feel like the Team from last season, in a bad way. They lack offense. Unfortunately, the 2x Smoke and 2x Wall of Fire were not helpful in this game, tho 2x Shatter staved off the inevitable. Tough loss.

10. No contest, they trot out 1-2-3 Vise, then wear down a Shield Spear with two Factory and a STD.

Machine. No mana, this Team needs some adjusting.

Doom. Great win, the twin Sylvan Library come up huge. They leveraged their 5-card draw to pluck back-to-back Hurricane to knock out 2x Birds and 2x Serra Angel. A Wolly Spider and an Elf do the rest.

YCBI. Unlucky, they win this game if not for the double-Library.

X. Barely a contest in this one. Two major cards played to gain advantage over FD; Disenchant on a Mana Flare & Balance to knock out three lands. The latter put the pin in the coffin as Lhurgoyf and Balduvian Horde joined a Kird Ape to finish things off. X much quicker than FD.

FD. Feels like any M-Kill whatsoever would put this team in their grave. So many cards just aren't that powerful unless a plethora of mana is on the table.

NLFY. Home team wins again, this time No Landia thriving on a first turn Black Vise and two quick M-Kill cards. One more Vise and a Black Knight helped finish things off in what felt like a no contest win.

DnS. Never got going, had a Shatter in hand but didn't have the mana to summon it. Three Rukh Egg bloated their hand. Not looking great here.

10. Take advantage of opposing mana struggles with a first turn Black Vise, and somehow win in 5 turns despite only two mana!

Things. Mana shortage against Vice = loss

YCBI. Nice win, they stuffed the Doom offense with superior defense and pretty much skated to victory. A later game Disenchant on a Gaea's Touch was critical in paving the way for 2x Archangel.

Doom. Mana struggles and a insufficient offense made this a no contest

DnS. Hell of a win, and nearly entirely due to Wheel of Fortune played on turn-14 to give way to more mana and 3x DD spells. They win only be 7 after being up 20-6 midway thru the game. Felt like this game had three distinct periods with two of them won by DnS. Had 4x lands destroyed and 2x Swampified. Great series-defining win.

NLFY. Sort of a gut wrenching loss here. They could take a 3-1 lead but instead lose a great deal of momentum after getting smacked here. The late WoF absolutely swung the game. There the kitchen sink out there this game, killing mana, using N-Disk, getting creatures out. Just wasn't enough!

Things. Fantastic win featuring the new draw advantage strengths this team now possesses. They about a double Vise with a Demonic Tutor - Hidden Horror - Recurring Nightmare after a Wheel of Fortune, which lands there team captain; SotN. Who's contending with that?! Yowza.

10. Had the double Vise but kind of have to give this one up to the Things.

Monsters. Win a slog of a game, relied heavily on Scroll Rack to recycle their way to the Mask-Nought combo. Pretty unexciting series, but it didn't feel like the Monsters looked that great either.

Gloves. End their season with two frustrating losses in a row. This game saw the downside of their strategy, as no offense could be found while the Monsters sat patiently, waiting to find their winning duo. Unfortunately, the Gloves strategy aligns all too nicely with the Monsters and were strapped as a result. Tough matchup. Tough season.

YCBI. Wow, fantastic win by YCBI to take the series lead at 3-2. Counterspell cleared the way after nixing a Overrun that would've raised hell with 4x 4/4 and one 6/7. Later, a Wrath of God cleared the board of nearly everything Darwin had thanks to the Living Lands in play. They withstood the bomb, and pecked their way to victory.

Doom. Made some ballsy moves by playing Living Lands. It left them completely exposed to the one card that couldn't hurt them, and of course YCBI draws it.

NLFY. Home team again, that's every game this series! No Landia was flush with mana this game and it made all the difference. Destroyed two Mountain,trotted out the S-Vampire and 2x Black Knight. Easy win.

DnS. If they keep both lost Mountain they win. Had thrown 3x DD spells out and sat on 4x Incinerate, a Fireball and a Wheel of Fortune.

10. The stuff off beauty here, going Black Vise, Winter Orb, Birds of Paradise and Unholy Strength for the quick win.

Things. This is the gameplay that the Things will struggle with; early Orb with no defense.

Doom. Colossal win to keep their championship dreams alive! They marched out 2x F-Elves, a Woolly Spider and a Ranger that was just a little too scary for YCBI. A Wrath of God came the next turn. Perhaps a bit presumptuous by the Commish! Either way, the Doom quickly had 2x Elves back on the board, a 5x Hurricane to knock out an opposing Archangel and Wall of Swords, then 2x Giant Growth to follow for the win. Game 7 at home looms!!

YCBI. Were feeling themselves after the WoG into Archangel combo, but the Doom LUCKILY nab a Forest on their next draw to power the game saving Hurricane.

NLFY. Victory! They break the streak by winning on DnS turf! A first turn Black Vise and a second turn Cyclopean Tomb was the winning formula. Cyclopean Tomb specifically just decimated the smoke makes this series. They kept DnS from summoning several more DD spells that, in fact, would've swung the game.

DnS. That's it, go down swinging. They lobbed 5x DD spells at their opponent, sat on a few more and just ran out of runway. Something if still missing from this team. It just doesn't feel like this is coming together for them. They don't really have a secondary strategy, I guess? Hard to say, but they were pinned down against the NLFY squad in a pretty tough way.

Things. Take Game 6 and the series with their patented combo thanks very much to a LDV and Dark Ritual. LDV looking like an early on favorite for postseason MVP.

10. Honestly, what a strange season they had. Early momentum, continued strength mid-season then a fall from grace and a first round exit. This is a good team but they're clearly not quite there.

YCBI. Kicking ass and taking names!! This YCBI team just handled the Doom in the series finale. Their twin Archangel made a huge difference in the outcome. It pains me to say it, but YCBI might be good!!!

Doom. What can I say, it was an underwhelming finish to their season. They drew a tough matchup with YCBI, as their speed was often negated by solid creature defense in the form of Meekstone, Marble Titan or Wall of Swords. They feel a little disappointed to me somehow. Wish they had win this series as I really like the Living Lands + Overrun additions.

Things. Smackdown. Three mana give way to a SotN with U-Mutation via Necromancy and Buried Alive. Lim-Dul's Vault is a fun card for this deck.

Doom. End the game with 6 lands, 3x Gaea's Touch, an Elves and a City of Solitude. If this deck is going for mana-surplus then I may need to make some changes.

SP. No contest thanks to Land Tax. Pulled three lands up to put 2x Crusade in play pumping up. A wolves and 2x Knight.

Hot. Ton of mana but little defense. Might need more counter defense in this deck.

Sliver. What a tough team. They just throw it all out at you if you give em long enough.

TF. Hung int there for a while but lost their major advantage with Hecatomb in turn-6 and didn't recover.

Fault. Damn, now this is the Team I remember from Season 5!!! They drew well and blew the absolute doors off of their nemesis. Destroyed two lands, killed their own Rukh Egg for the flyer, then put two Shocker in place with a Megrim to follow. A whopping 27-damage was done on the final turn; 2-Shocker, 5-Rukh, 24-Megrim. Yeeeeesh.

Gloves. Were mana stuck early and never recovered.

Monsters. I love this team. Methodically, artistically and effectively staved off the Birds for long enough to get two Nought out for 20 damage. Beautiful.

YCBI. Lost four c I tidal lands in this game thanks to Grindstone and Ray of Erasure. From turn-7 onward they were stuck with three lands in play and four MCC cards in hand. Brutal.

DnS. Storm's 'a brewin!!! The Stormy take full advantage of a mana-stalled No Landia by dousing them in flames on turns-5 & 6 via 12x from Ball Lightning and 10x from Fireball (thanks to a Furnace of Rath in play).

NLFY. No mana outside of a single Quicksand.

X. A game they should've won, and did, but thanks primarily to Disenchant. They drew and used 3x of them to dispatch an early N-Disk and 2x Jade Statue. It made all the difference in enabling a Ape/Lions combo to wreak havoc.

Fax. Closer! Love Jade Statue and Juggernaut was fun. Had 2x Jester's Cap in hand and didn't know what to do with them. I suppose getting them out early to remove Disenchant would be the right way to play them. Could use another N-Disk in here for defense. Liked seeing a near-Urzatron on the board, too.

Team 10. Best win of the season. The advantage was shifting to FD around mid-game when a Builder's Bane destroyed a Vise and Relic Barrier. The Barrier specifically was wreaking havoc on the FD Mana Vault, but it left FD vulnerable to Winter Orb, which came a few turns later. Once out, 10 was able to summon at-will thanks to a Sol Ring and 2x Birds and won thanks to a Mishra's Factory. Great win for what looks to be a very strong team.

FD. Brutal. Had a W in their sights once the Relic Barrier was removed, but the Orb threw out their game plan. The six mana in play went useless, as did their Vault.

YCBI. On a roll baby! 4x birds squawk their opponent to death with help from big boy Archangel himself. Kind of an easy win, actually.

X. Looking pedestrian for the first time in a long time. Maybe it was the three weeks off? No answer for the winged foes.

Slivers. Damn that was fast. In 5-turns, the Slivers had four mana, 5x creatures on the battlefield, received no damage and already dealt 27-damage. This is the third time these two are meeting in the playoffs with the Slivers mopping the floor with the Things last season; 3-0. Maybe they've got their number?

Things. Had the right cards in hand but were too slow to contend with the ridiculous speed of the Slivers.

SP. No contest win, looked fast with 2x White Knight and a Tundra Wolves out there. The 7 to 1 deployed cards differential tells the whole story.

NLFY. One mana, a Strip Mine, for most of the game and a bloated hand (3x Sengir Vampire, Demonic Hordes, N-Disk, Hellfire).

Monsters. An incredibly rare 3-turn win thanks the ideal draw of one mana, a Sol Ring, 2x Nought and a turn-1 Mask. Two turns later yielded 24 damage.

Fax. Lose an impossible game. Not worried about it 🤪

YCBI. Starting to feel like their series, although they win this one entirely due to mana issues. Still, they're getting out on the offensive and X isn't.

X. Green hand without any Forest. Down 2-0 suddenly.

Slivers. Again winning in 5-turns. This isn't even on the Things, as the Slivers summoned 4x creatures on three mana with another 4x creatures ready to go in-hand.

Things. Speed is one thing, but mistakenly grabbing the Kraken vs. the Spirit of the Night was just foolish.The Kraken comes out and Lining immediately drop a Winged Sliver to fly right by the big guy.

NLFY. A slow, methodical win to even the series. They chipped away early on 2x Strip Mine, then knocked out three more with a Demonic Hordes. The Black Knight was key, too. Dark Ritual gets MVP for putting the Hordes out early.

SP. Struggled really to draw more mana, and ended up losing 5 total in the game.

Monsters. Another great draw, this time starting with the Mask and pulling the Nought out of the deck on turn-2. Unless the opponent is stocked with A-Kill or C-Kill, the Monsters just aren't going to lose a game like this.

Fax. Man, another one? Almost had enough defense on the battlefield to contend with the big guy. Maze of Ith also the perfect foil to Juggernaut.

X. Big win and largely uncontested. Two Hurricane and 2x Lightning Bolt really sealed the deal in this one. X don't feel like they have a go to standalone creature. A few times now Worldly Tutor was used on a Djinn that becomes moot a turn later. Wonder if they need a wild card.

YCBI. White hand with all Islands.

Slivers. Someone pissed them off, make it 3rd consecutive win in 5-turns. That's got to be a record. They are just pummeling the Things.

Things. Their strategy is there, the cards look good, and the game play has been fine, but it's just not fast enough for this Lining team.

Potatoes. Home team wins again! This game took on a very defensive flavor with 21 turns needed to capture the W. The Potatoes lost 10x lands and 5x creatures in this game. They evaded a Black Vise for the most part and chipped away. Hard fought victory.

NLFY. Never quite got there, digging deep into their mana supply to deploy cards thanks to Lake of the Dead.

Fax. Chip, chip, chipping away in this game to outlast the Monsters. They're key to victory in this series is to overwhelm big blue with creatures and they were able to do that this game despite losing 2x to Control Magic and hitting resistance in 2x Maze of Ith. Staying alive!

Monsters. No momentum this game, but enough D to stave off the L for a while..

X. Tie up the series thanks to Wrath of God and a Cataclysm. Ample mana, early 2x Kird Ape, a Djinn and a Lhurgoyf was the recipe. Worldly Tutor used again this game. Really should be for something with haste.

YCBI. Another imbalanced game leaving 4x cards in hand despite 6x lands in play (5 Plains).

Things. Staying alive! What an absolute beat down!! Only three mana, the Things use a Feminist Demonic Tutor for a Buried Alive, then use a Dark Ritual for a Living Death to put 2x Crimson Hellkite and a Spirit of the Night into play for the easy W.

Slivers. Were looking good early but had their doors blown off.

Potatoes. Took their time, withstood early slugging by Vise and crossed to victory in full control. They are not scared of this NLFY team.

NLFY. Strange game, draw 3x Vise but only one land! So they deal 13x damage early on them absolutely sputter. Elimination game up next..

Monsters. Doozy of a game, Monsters come back from being down roughly 16 to 2 thanks to 2x T-P Box. The 3x Maze of Ith saved the game from being over about 10-12 turns earlier. Monsters were lucky to win this game.

Fax. Can't believe they drop this one, with such a sizable lead and creatures galore. Puts them in a deep hole heading into Game 5.

X. Surgical win to take their third in a row and with that, the lead in this series. Critical to the W was the early Cataclysm to knock out 3x lands from YCBI. Two Hurricane were huge as well. Still missing an early Mirri, Cat Warrior type though.

YCBI. Were just a tad show this game, likely due to the Cataclysm.

Slivers. Bingo! There's the series, the Slivers look incredible, winning for the fourth time in only 5-turns. Just unbelievable speed. Might be a all-time honestly. Even more incredible is doing with on two-then-one lands.

Things. Never had a chance in this one, and, really, the series. Losing in only 5 turns for separate times is absurd. The one bit of solace they can take in this loss is the promise of their revamped roster heading into the off-season. It was a natural for for them and the addition of draw advantage cards for their scheme seamlessly.

Potatoes. Win in fitting fashion - a disrespectful, brazen march to victory at the hands of Armageddon. Once again they were patient and it paid off. I really dislike how good this team is, and even thought about removing them from next season MID-GAME just to avoid playing with them! Onto the semi-finals for Smashed Potatoes.

NLFY. Still not built to beat this team! The past two games they were laden with high MCC cards - Demonic Tutor, Sengir Vampire, Hellfire. It'd feel odd to rip them out, but playing these cards may require more mana boosters? Either way, this roster just isn't getting it done.

Monsters. The 3x successive Maze of Ith came up huge against a really fast, formidable offensive from Fax. Just as Colossus of Sardia touched down, though, the Monsters used a timely Control Magic to snatch the big brute. The ensuing turn, Mist drew a Nought and summoned a second one thanks to Transmute Artifact. Suddenly Fax was looking at a 9/9 and 2x 12/12. The big guys arched the following turn, knocked out every opposing creature, then used a Reconstruction to put the big boy back on the battlefield for the win. Really looked good in this game, balanced. They advance to the semifinals.

Fax. Lose in five, which doesn't feel great after the regular season run they had, but 2 of those losses were insanely unfortunate, one was down to the wire and the last was also hard fought. They have an identity, though, and something fun to build on entering their first full season as an artifact only squad. Pretty cool.

YCBI. Ladies and gentlemen, we have a Game 7! YCBI went all out in this game throwing several birds on the offensive to claim the W. This team had a fighting chance against opponents that (a) do not feature mana kill and (b) do not feature flying creatures. They just so happen to face a third opponent on a row with those characteristics.

X. Didn't draw well in this game. They've used Worldly Tutor to pull a Djinn for the last time. One attack and frozen by Meekstone. It's just enough already! 

My money is on YCBI in the finale.

X. Damn, very close to becoming only the second #1 seed ever to lose in the first round of premier bracket. A turn-1 Taiga propel them the victory by enabling 2x Kird Ape to start pounding away. A later Hurricane sealed the deal. Onto the next one!

YCBI. A storied run comes to an end behind an uninspiring finish, leaving them one mana short before some bigger defense would've come into play. Still, an incredibly exciting run by Dad's homage team here. Really cool too see them take the top dog to 7 games.

Birds. Beautiful comeback! After destroying a pumped up Spirit of the Night with a game-saving Wrath of God, the Birds flew into the W thanks to a Serra Angel. They were down 16-4. A really nice win!

Things. Were FAST out the gate with their SotNight out on turn-2 thanks to Dark Ritual 2x and a Buried Alive - Necromancy combo. An Unstable Mutation put this game near the finish line but they couldn't hold on.

Potatoes. One of the wildest wins in history...
- 32 turns
- 65 total damage dealt
- 30 total cards left
- 12 direct damage spells cast
- 3 Armageddon and 2 Cleansing cast
- 40 cards played
...resulting in a 21 to -6 victory for the defending champs. Wow.

DnS. Never felt like they would win this game with Land Tax and Zuran Orb in play.

Sliver. Seemed like a lucky win, as they trotted out 8 Slivers in this game. They used a Worldly Tutor to grab a Muscle Sliver mid-game and it made all the difference. They also drew a Heart Sliver on their last turn to grab the W. Nice win against a really good X team.

X. They are right there. This is the type of game they need to start winning if they're to make a leap into the elite. I used Giant Growth on the offensive too early, two of them, when they should have been preserved for defense against an all-offense team. Or, I should have used the Balduvian Horde earlier, but I was afraid of losing the Wrath of God. I did lose the WoG, but never drew the Plains anyway. Tough loss, right there.

T-10. I love this team, and man are they competitive. In only 5 turns they put out 8 cards on five mana; 2x BoP, 1x STD, 2x Unholy Strength, 2x Black Vise, 1x Winter Orb. Their roster just makes so much sense. A Team that used to lose because they were "too much work" suddenly seems painfully simple. It's fantastic. This time beating up the Gloves.

Gloves. Clunky. They started strong with a 1st-turn U-Dreams, then a Howling Mine to follow but sputtered as soon as a D-Tutor was cast without any idea what to bring up. The inclusion of blue hindered them this game as they got cut up by their own City of Brass. Might need to start re-thinking that color addition.

Fax. First win!! Primary thanks go to Juggernaut who deals all but 3 damage in this game. Not much resistance.

Hot. Psychic Venom is a good card. Need to pair with Mana Short and maybe Icy Manipulator.

FD. Mana, and oodles of it. They pump out 8 mana in 9-turns to them all the DD flexibility in the world. A mid-game Thunder knocked out three Elves from DD and that was the beginning of the end.

Doom. Looked good early was tons of mana but didn't draw much offense the rest of the game.

NLFY. Quick win. Turn-1 Black Vise into turn-2 Icequake to destroy a crucial land. Another Vise and Vampire to follow.

Fault. Lost a crucial land early and never recovered.

Monsters. Wow, BARELY win this game but thanks to 2x Counterspell and a Maze of Ith, they emerge with a deceivingly high margin of victory. An early Kormus Bell was countered, then, on turn-7, a nearly game-winning Drain Life was played and countered to preserve the win. Had they not countered the Drain Life, they would've been down 1 life.

Thrulls. WOW, were they close. With two of the games biggest creatures, they still had a chance. On their last turn, they drew the perfect card - Dark Ritual - to bring them close to victory but Maze of Ith and a Counterspell were there to play defense.

Monsters. Big win done in strange fashion. They were sitting ducks for the first handful of turns with only one mana. Finally the Scroll Rack enabled some flow and it was only a matter of time before they put their Dreadnought tanks on the battlefield. The beauty of this win was the key roles played by Field of Dreams, Millstone and Maze of Ith. That trio held X at bay, and also allowed the Monsters to prepare against a Wrath of God (that they countered). Great win really.

X. Showing a real lack of firepower recently. Feels like their stable of Kird Ape and Erhnam Djinn just isn't cutting, dating back to last series against YCBI. They could really use some additional heft on the roster. They might've taken this game w/o 2x Maze of Ith stifling all attack.

Sliver. Take Game 1 behind some swift offense via Muscle Sliver and 2x Crystalline. Easy victory.

Potatoes. Might be in trouble in this series as they really don't have fast defenders to throw into the battlefield. Wall of Swords is a great one but he might be too slow?

Monsters. Gut this one out, thanks to two critical Resurrection - both used to save a Illusionary Mask. Otherwise, they didn't have much going, taking 11 damage on the chin early thanks to an undefended Kird Ape. Millstone knocked off a Cataclysm in wonderful D.

X. Lackluster again, this time drawing far too many lands with little to summon. They need more creatures.

Slivers. Grit, grit and more grit help the Slivers hang on for the Game 2 victory. It takes them 24 bruising turns to do so and 49 total damage. Damn. Might be a record. Needed 21 cards to win this one, including the MVP Sliver Queen!

Potatoes. Man, if they're not winning this type of game then they might be in trouble. Drag it out, rely on the D, but the offense wasn't there. They dealt only 10-damage in in this game - a glaring weakness to the Commish.

Monsters. Win a bruiser to take a 3-0 series lead. Pretty impressive win, holding on for the first ~10 turns as twin Kird Ape ran amok. They were easily down double-digit points before their draw engine started bearing fruit. Scroll Rack is almost unfair and, yet, perfectly complementary to this roster - using mill abilities to seek out their dynamic duo in Mask-Nought. This game was no different. They somehow survived an apocalypse 3x; 2x Wrath of God and a Cataclysm. 

X. If only they had a little more early firepower they could have taken this one. The early Ape duo is fine, but it shouldn’t be counted upon to win a game outright. No way. They needed the next level of offensive depth in the form of a Djinn or something else. That seems to be the story here time and time again, though. That said, their defense was spectacular. To avoid a sweep, they’re going to need a Djinn in the first 3-4 turns, I suspect.

Slivers. Make it 3-in-a-row now, taking this one in a Sliver-paced 7-turns. Even still, they needed to dole out more than double the normal amount of damage to win (42) thanks to a Zuran Orb in play. A turn-4 Disenchant on a Land Tax was critical, however. One more to go!

Potatoes. Felt quickly lost in the shuffle in this one.

Extinction. Oh yeah! They stay alive with big time victory featuring 3x Lhurgoyf. They benefited from a mid-game Cataclysm that wiped out 2x Maze of Ith and 3x Islands. The Nought’s were coming, too, and a turn before saw one of them Disenchanted. Good drawing by X. The finishing move came with the aforementioned Cataclysm when a Angelic Renewal enabled the second Lhurgoyf to stay on the  ‘field. Buckets!

Monsters. A lot of drilling with no oil.

Slivers. Look at this! The improbable sweep against the reigning champ! Not to mention, they win the clincher on the Potatoes terms - taking 19 turns to earn the W. This was similar to Game 2 in that they were forced to chip away little by little as the Potatoes searched for creature defense. Disenchant takes the Game MVP for the second time by removing a Land Tax and preventing the Potatoes from gaining ground.

Potatoes. Wow, kind of speechless here. What a disappointing end to Season 7, stumbling into the playoffs and out of it. Slivers might just be a tough match-up for them as their speed invalidated the Potatoes defensive schemes.

X. Staying alive! Make it two now, and they win this one with more patience and precision. The bevy of defensive cards at their disposal is a tough matchup for the Monsters. Multiple Disenchant, a Cataclysm and more in hand to protect themselves against the mighty 12/12.

Monsters. It's interesting, the Monsters really only have one creature on the roster. As a result, X can sit back and stockpile arrows to unleash on the big guy whenever he shows up. Workerd in this one.

Monsters. And that's a wrap!! The Monsters devour Extinction in Game 6 to take the series behind some nifty draw engines in Teferi's Puzzle Box and Transmute Artifact. The big guy made it out and that was that.

X. I enjoyed the fight in this team, taking Games 4 & 5, and this game was no different. They destroyed the Mask twice and were then left for dead once it came back a third time. Great season by this squad.

Potatoes. Early mana struggles had them buried early but a full remind came on the heels of two Disenchant and a Crusade to keep the Potatoes undefeated.

10. Looked completely lackluster on offense this game. Had ten mana at their disposal but nothing to summon. Ankh of Mishra is a great card, works really well in here and is downright treacherous for these Potatoes. First loss of the season against the only undefeated team left.

Sliver. Feels like they were toast for a while, but outlasted the Birds in a great win. They sat tight waiting for more lands and the Birds never brought anymore offense outside of one Serra. Two Coat of Arms were destroyed, and one more summoned via Enlightened Tutor. Once in the field, the Slivers were too pumped up to lose.

Birds. Looked real good early, freezing out the Slivers with a Wrath of God, then a Meekstone and Kismet. But once their Serra was killed off and the Meekstone lost, they lacked any significant creatures defense or offense. But hung in there against a stalwart.

Dark. Smokey smoke. A Ball Lightning and 4x DD spells for a 4-turn win. Yeesh.

Gloves. Nothing.

NLFY. Took hits early from Dark Maze and Walking Wall, but leaned on Black Vise to outlast the Hot. They sat patiently, used an 8er Drain Life and won.

Hot. Some moves early on but it kind of stinks when Manabarbs hits the field. They just sit there uneventfully. Might not be the "build-around" card I thought it was.

Doom. Erase a two-game losing streak by BLANKING the Monsters. An early Crumble erased a I-Mask and completely changed the game. They were so fast in this game, putting a Djinn, FoN, Ranger and a F-Elves into play on offense.

Monsters. No chemistry in this game, needed either FoDreams or a Nought.

Fault. Win in 6 turns thanks to a double Megrim and Shocker fooling out 29-damage in one shot. Two key lands we're destroyed as well.

Fax. Eeks. No Mana and nothing to summon.

Thrull. Exceptional! Hecatomb is a beast. Really the first time I've seen it win a game like this. FD wins the next turn, but two Sengir Autocrat enable victory.

FD. Three Mana Vault and seven mana gave them a TON to work with, even a Crimson Hellkite made it out!! They poured 18-damage down in their final turn and it just wasn't enough.

Slivers. Skate to a victor thanks to Crystalline Sliver which immediately neutralized the Maze of Ith in play.

Monsters. Crystalline Sliver is a problem!

Slivers. Cooking up the Monsters here. In just 4-turns they have 6x creatures in play dealing 20x damage. Interesting note, they were able to do this on only 3 lands! Very few teams are able to deploy that many cards with so little mana in play & do that much damage. Pretty impressive and a unique strength of this team.

Monsters. Second game in a row with surplus of mana drawn. This time it was 11 of 20, leaving the Monsters completely defenseless (Maze of Ith neutralized again by Crystalline Sliver). The Slivers might be uncovering a critical flaw of the Monsters roster; no real creature defense outside of Maze of Ith.

Monsters. Look out! The Monsters, on the brink of destruction, pull back-to-back Phyrexian Dreadnought, then use Control Magic to steal the newly entered Sliver Queen for the comeback victory. They were down 20 to 8 before storming back in this one.

Slivers. Maybe too cute in this game. Obviously had the lead, then chose to not defend the attacking Nought so they could summon Sliver Queen the next turn for defense and then follow with either another Queen or a Coat of Arms. That said, the chances of consecutive draws of the Big Boy were not very likely!!!!

Slivers. One game away from their first ever title, this time leaning on 4x City of Brass to dispatch 5x Slivers (Victual, Crystalline, Muscle, Heart & Metallic) for the victory. Never seen them put all City of Brass in play, was actually kind of cool. This game was largely uncontested though they did use 2x Swords to Plowshares to take care of a mid-game Nought. Now leading 3-1, they'll have to win only one of three to take home the crown.

Monsters. Looked thin again in this game. Summoning 8x cards didn't do much for them offensively or defensively, and the two Maze of Ith were neutralized early on. There were 2x Millstone grinding away at their own library searching for a second Nought but they came up empty.

Monsters. Not done yet! Take this game by storm with 3x Nought on turn-3 to block the Sliver win (killed off 3x attackers) and dole out a jaw-dropping 36x damage the next turn.

Slivers. Great draw, put 6x slivers out on the field and had the win locked up save for the last minute blockade of Noughts. May have given the Monsters life here...

Slivers. CHAMPIONS AT LAST!!!!!! Game 6 saw them steamroll to victory behind a plethora of mana and 4x Slivers (Metallic, Muscle, Crystalline, Winged) and a Coat of Arms. It took them seven seasons, but alas the Slivers have prevailed landing their first ever championship.

Monsters. A very strong run to the Finals but their final two losses highlighted major weaknesses of the group; they can often go entire games without amassing much O or D to put pressure on opponents. Perhaps there's a way to resolve in the off-season so they're more ironclad come Season 8.

Birds. Totally wacky game, the balance shifted from team to team several times. Landing a Soraya was huge to pump up two other falcons. An additional Marble Titan helped them escape with the win. Interesting to see a team built to disable creatures best a team without any creatures!

Gloves. Lose with 4x U-Dreams in play. They kept pushing to outlast the Birds and sacrificed creature defense along the way.

Things. Two early Hidden Horror really amok.

DnS. One mana, no contest.

Fax. Oh Yeah! So fun! Had Urzatron out there, a Desert and a M-Factory for mana, then a Colossus of Sardia, a Brass Man and a Xanthic Statue to lay waste to the Doom.

Doom. Barely hung on, hard to say but it looks like Kudzu might have hurt them this game.

Fault. Win a fantastic game here. They made a few ballsy calls and were nearly dead when they drew a Mind Twist to deal the final 4 points of damage to FD to win the game.

Drillers. Really unfortunate loss here, incurring a 1x mana burn to summon a Shivan and end up losing two turns later.

Boom. WHOA BABY! A nice win putting their core strategy into action -- 2x Portcullis lead the T-Song-activated charge with supplementary help from a Psychic Venom early on. A Mana Leak wins MVP for dispatching a Nought early on which altered the entire game.

Monsters. Using a Transmute Artifact to put a I-Mask into play with a Nough to follow on turn-2/3 is just bliss. It's too bad the Nought was countered, otherwise this is another win in the books. Should be mentioned that the Monsters countered 2x T-Song but the second one was countered by a Avoid Fate.

Potatoes. I dont like this Team, but man was that a surgical win. They jumped out to a quick lead only to see three unchallenged creatures go to the graveyard. Two Armageddon stripped any hope from X who had 3x Lhurgoyf in-hand with two more creature sweepers. So surgical and nicely done.

X. Wow, what a tough loss. They were one mana away from taking hold of this thing - twice. They really do put some pressure on the Potatoes though. These match-ups are among the most enjoyable in the league.

Sliver. What a beat-down. Simple as that, and I'm shocked. T-10 had a Winter Orb out, 2x BoP for mana and a Treefolk in-hand but had their doors totally blown off by 6x Sliver cards, including a Queen. It was the Coat of Arms that created the life points chasm with 26-damage in the final turn.

T-10. Out-creatured here. Slivers had the right mana to a bevy of creatures into play - nearly half their drawn cards actually. T-10 doesn't have the defense to contend with that kind of firepower - they need to get out in front to control.

NLFY. No contest. Thrulls sacrifice creatures to stay under the Black Vise tax and spin out as a result. A Sengir Vampire deals most of the remaining damage, and a Drain Life adds to the fun.

Thrulls. Slapped upside the head.

Slivers. Come out swinging baby! The only way they win this series is (a) with speed or (b) with several Coat of Arms on the battlefield and even that may backfire. LFG!

Boss Fight. Omnath changes the whole game, but tip their hat to the winner in this one.

Boss. Destructive in this one to get the W! Elves, Insects and Wurms galore to deal a 31-damage in the final turn. Hornet Queen is an awesome card, and I was on the fence about it early on. I also really enjoyed pairing Living Hive with the Queen. Kindercatch has fantastic artwork. Worldspine Wurm even made it out - much in thanks to the Priest of Titania and the Llanowar Elves. 

Slivers. No chance they win a game like this one. Just far too much firepower on the other side of the battlefield. Even a Coat of Arms wouldn’t have helped...it would’ve aided the Boss!!!!!

Monsters. Slow win, but outdraw BAF en route to a Mask-Nought combo-led victory. FoD is super fun, and I love the control element once it's in play with Sindbad, Ray of Erasure and Grind/Millstone's around it. So fun. This win broke a record tie in the standings, and ended a 2-game losing streak.

BAF. Never got going in this one. The loss ends a 2-game win streak.

Potatoes. Armageddon is almost unfair sometimes. It's just so demonstrative! Post-play, the Potatoes slide to victory easily.

YCBI. Had some early momentum and a pathway to the W until the 'geddon. Couldn't recover afterward.

Machine. Nice core win!! Urzatron leads to a Xanthic Statue and a pumped Mindless Automaton.

Frottle. Two Hecatomb make it out on 4x Mana and it still isn't enough to cut the Machine's life in half. Not good.

FD. Blow out. Mana advantage wins them the game. Detonate made a critical showing by taking out an Icy, helping to turn the game in FDs favor for good.

Boom. No protection against flying creatures, and certainly not against the 12pt Fireball, with only two mana.

Doom. Yeahhhhh baby! The Doom are back!! One Fyndhorn Elf takes on the all-blacks with multiple Giant Growth and an Overrun. I love Overrun, such a great green card. The Sylvan Library enables three additional draw cards that eventually ended the game, thanks to a pumped up Hurricane. Still doesn't feel like they have enough creatures, and they certainly benefited from a mana stalled NLFY.

NLFY. No room to run in this one.

Slivers. Win by a hair! Use 3x CoArms to pump up their only two creatures; Metallic & Muscle to victory. A nice win against long-time foe DnS.

DnS. Go on the offensive, using a Lightning Bolt early in the game vs. saving it for defense. What happens? A Muscle Sliver comes alive the next turn and totally changes the game. Unreal.

10. Toughest win so far, but man is this team fun. Finally, a Wurmwood Treefolk dominance, utilizing the Swampwalk ability to skate by the Spirit of the Night stalking the sidelines. That was the key to victory after some fancy mana play; BoP, Fellwar Stone and Mishra's Factory.

Things. Honestly don't know how they were even in this game with the cards they drew. A SotN hit the battlefield but they need him on D. Otherwise, they had nothing going. Tombstone Stairwell made an appearance and I love that card - don't know that I'm using it correctly but I do love it.

Monsters. Great win despite losing some control early. I love playing with this roster, it's BAF-level synergy. Transmute Artifact is an incredible card. I love the Mask-Nought combo and seeing Millstone.

Gloves. So close in this one. If they remove the Nought, they win.

Boom. Oh yeah baby! Titania's Song comes to life!! The key difference maker was a Winter's Blast to kill off three opposing Falcon. Nice win by the Boom.

YCBI. Fighting with 1/1 creatures is an uphill battle, and they lost this one.

DnS. Fantastic win, finally looked like themselves. Loved the impact of Boisum Strip - resurrecting two DD for the W. They laid out 8x smoke makers in this game. Looked very fast. Real fun win.

Doom. Love the Overrun addition and feels right, though there's still some offense missing from this team.

X. Taiga wins this game with Kird Ape + 2x Giant Growth. They cruise to victory behind a S-Lions after Pox knocked out the Ape.

Fault. Lackluster performance. They had no defense for Ape or Lions and went down easily.

NLFY. Great win. They beat this team in 6 in the semis of the playoffs last season, and used the same playbook to halt their 5-game win streak; go after the mana, slow things down and gradually assert will. Worked again, as they used a t-2 Tutor to pull a Swamp, of all things, to destroy a land the next turn, then put a Sol Ring into play which gave light to N-Disk, a Sengir and two more M-Killers. Really nice win.

Slivers. Suddenly can't get over the hump against this team. A lost mana early hurt them badly, as did losing a Crystalline and Metallic Sliver to the N-Disk.

Potatoes. Man, this team is solid. Both times they looked to be losing control, they nailed FD with a Disenchant on a Mana Flare. An early Armageddon helped their control play, and they didn't really assert much offense on FD otherwise.

FD. Funny how a Mana Vault can appear like it's unlocking possibility and one turn later it's crushing you. I like that element of the card, forces smart play, and boy was it missing in this game. Not really, but when both Mana Flare were destroyed, FD sat and bled at the hands of 2x untapped Vault's. Brutal.

Fax. Awesome! T-2 Scorched Ruins put a Juggernaut in play - loved that sequence to put immediate pressure on the Things. A few turns later the Urzatron was in play letting Xanthic Statue loose with Triskelion, Colossus, Flowstone Sculpture and Mindless Automaton not far behind. So fun to see them with 12 mana on 5 lands - pretty sick. 

Things. Clunky draw and terrible play of Lim Dul's Vault put them in a rut. Just didn't have it this game.

10. A fantastic, grit your teeth victory. They take care of not one but two Lord of the Pit in this game, somehow, and succeed on the heels of Mr. Yawggy himself. So fun.

Thrulls. This is about as good as they could look and they were just flat out beaten. Did beg the question about whether or not they should focus entirely on getting the big guy out there.

Gloves. Erase a 6-game losing streak!!! They threw 4x Howling Mine and 2x Underworld dreams out there - not sure anyone's walking away from that combination. Yeeesh.

FD. Uh oh. They looked terrible for the second straight game and almost entirely due to Mana Vault. I'm really not sure I'm playing them correctly, but it's the first time this season I felt like they were too flawed a team to realistically compete, and this was against the 14th place team...

Fault. Ugly win, land 12 damage via Megrim-WoF early and really have no other offense until a Shocker & Specter deal the remaining 8 with three damage at a time. Dark Ritual, Rukh Egg don't make any sense in this deck.


YCBI. The WoF crushed them, as did a Mind Twist later on.

DnS. Fantastic win. They are back to their roots laying waste turn by turn by turn despite having only 2-3 mana for most of the game. Not many teams can deploy 13 cards in such little mana supply. Smoke was demonstrative here and totally validated their inclusion in the roster - freezing out the Kormus Bell from TF.

Frottle. Another tough loss. They waited patiently to throw a massive 14pt haymaker via Drain Life on t-10 but were stuck from the Smoke otherwise.

X. Pulls this one out by the skin of their teeth with a lucky, final draw in L-Bolt to deal 7-damage alongside E-Djinn for the W. The turn prior, they used a Disenchant to destroy an Icy, and pumped the Djinn with a GG. They threw 12 cards out in this game - >1 / turn. That's nuts. Actually liked sacrificing the zoo creatures (Lions, Birds, Apes) with a Lhurgoyf in hand even if he didn't make it out. Nice win.

Boom. Tough loss, though I like this team. The identity is definitely built around Portcullis / T-Song vs. the Psychic Venom-types like I had thought. Wonder if that's the most complementary sidekick - will have to noodle!

Fax. Make it 4!!! The win streak continues thanks to a total dud by the Slivers. That said, the Machine still threw out 3x Juggernaut & 3x Mishra's Factory to bitch slap the Slivers. Fantastic. Also, Mindless Automaton might not work in this deck.

Slivers. One land all game gives them their first losing streak of the season.

Monsters. Tested the Scroll Rack and DAMN. It wins MVP hands down, basically powered a search warrant for Dreadnought's - incredible. Combine this with the option to mill your own deck and wow. The draw advantage was insane in this game. Granted, Potatoes drew lands on 8 of 19 cards, but still.

Potatoes. First loss of the season. Very little offense and never drew a M-Kill card - unusual for them. The Maze of Ith really hurts them.

Things. Break consecutive losses with a win over the 5th place team to get back to .500 thanks in large part to Hidden Horror + 2x Unstable Mutation. The Underground Sea gets the nod, as it was responsible for the C-Pump that won them the game. I was close to breaking up the Things until this win. Maybe they're not done yet after all!

NLFY. Drop to 6-5 with this loss, slipping slowly out of the top echelon of teams. Had no answer for a black creature, as has been the case. It's one of the few sore spots for this team.

Doom. Fabulous! They trot out 7-lands in 6-turns, turn 'em all into Dendrites via Living Lands and attack the stuffing out of seemingly-unbeatable-Team 10. Just fabulous. What a great addition Living Lands is. Concordant Crossroads needs to be waived at the third-mark.

Team 10. Well, a Winter Orb would've turned this game on its' head but no dice! Instead they falter to what should be a bruiser in the second 2/3s of the season.

NLFY. Win an easy one here. The Gloves put forth little defense after stifling the already-knocked-your-socks-off-you're-welcome-Black Vise. A Sengir and Drain Life did the rest.

Gloves. Continue to look insanely brazen. That said, I still like them?

Fault. Wow, this Team is suddenly spilling over with swagger. They relied on new FAs Fire Ants, Lodestone Bauble and Blood Lust to chop out one leg of DnS after another. Very different dynamic and super fun. Love the potential for them with this new scheme.

DnS. Got smacked. Lost 6 Mountains in this game - 6!! Also lost a Rukh Egg, who would've been summoned immediately for a Rukh and some DD. The 2x Wall of Earth didn't help either;

X. Great win, no question. They were balanced on O&D, throwing 2x Disenchant and 2x Lightning Bolt to dispatch a Sol Ring, Sylvan Library, Elvish Ranger and Y-Ants. Critical to the win. So was their Erhnam Djinn, beneficiary of 2x Giant Growth and a board-clearing Cataclysm. They had a Lhurgoyf in-hand with 2x WoG, as well. Quite the war chest.

Doom. Very close, couldn't recover after the Cataclysm. Living Lands fell right into X's hands, so it went unplayed. Didn't have much else.

Sliver. Cast 2x Disenchant to remove a Mana Flare and a Power Surge from the field - change the game entirely. They coast to victory behind 5x Slivers (Muscle, Winged, Metallic, Acidic, Crystalline).

FD. Maybe they have a chance if the Mana Flare isn't destroyed? That said, likely not? They're just too damn slow!

Monsters. Hot damn. This game was a remake due to player error in the first, which looked like it tipped the scales from the Things to the Monsters. Only way to tell this game is from the opponent's POV...

Things. Fucking A. Had the triple-A bruisers on the field by turn-3 and yet still lost. Unbelievably bad luck. Their two Kraken got SHOWN THE FUCK UP by 2x Nought. What a scrap. First time in history a game has showcased the largest creatures in the format on one battlefield - let alone 2x of each. Insane! Alas, they lost to an I-Mask and 2x Nought fair and square. Ball don't lie.

Fax Machine. ye..yeee..yeeaa....yeahhh..YEEAHHHHHHHH BABY!!!!! Count it, 5 in a row for the MAAACHINNNNNNEEEEE. They move FAST in this game with a Sol Ring and a smorgasbord of lands to trot out a Juggernaut, Triskelion (killed off White Knight and Soul Lady), Automaton and a Xanthic Statue for the win. What a goddamn beatdown against the #1 seed.

Potatoes. Looked like a 13-seed in this game with nothing going. Get SLAPPED as a result. So fun :)

10. Just a matter of time in this game. They wore down Peak-a-Boom slowly and steadily with 2x STDs, a Elvish Archer and 2x M-Factory. A Relic Barrier - Winter Orb combo really helped disable any defense from the Boom. This team is really good.

Boom. This team stinks. Maybe it's impossible to build around Titania's Song..

YCBI. Ugliest game of the season. Birds sit tight on D until their 2x Serra Angel came through to turn up the heat. Their 3x Disenchant were critical in dispatching 2x Hecatomb and something else (?). Good win.

Thrulls. Were right there and very inventive in their gameplay, leveraging a Breeding Pit, Hecatomb and a Lord of the Pit pretty well in my opinion. Not a bad loss here.

10. A little bit more speed gets them the uncontested win here. Summoning six cards on three mana is damn good.

Fault. Little out of sorts here.

Gloves. What a great game this was. Gloves took an early lead with a first turn U-Dreams and a quickly 7/7 E-Spector following a WoF. They then started to lose ground due to multiple big burly Machines, but 2x WotW helped play defense. Howling Mine ramped up their offense. Then a N-Disk destroyed their whole squad. Eventually put another E-Spector out there and closed the win.

Machine. Tough loss, end their heroic 5 game winning streak. They might actually be good.

X. Awesome win. TF was right there with the Auto-Tomb combo but denied thanks to 2x Lightning Bolt and then a Wrath of God. A 5/6 Lhurgofy was blessed by a Cataclysm then it was just a matter of time.

TF. Really not bad. Had early momentum for their combo, spotted and immediately shifted to get a Lord of the Pit on the field. Didn't last, but the momentum was all there. Really nice to see. They just ran into a buzzsaw.

DnS. Close one, but 4x DD and one Ball Lightning did the trick. 

Boom. Had two 4/4s on the board thanks to T-Song. Had a chance to chip away but Smoke was showing them down considerably.

Potatoes. Cruise in this one after laying a Armageddon on t-4. They rode a pumped White Knight for the remainder of the game. Had ample defense in the form of 2x StPlowshares in-hand, a Land Tax - Zuran Orb on the board too.
