Freaks. Win the battle of the losing streaks, snapping a two-game slide thanks to 3x Flying Men and a critical Counterspell to dispatch an early Megrim. 

BAF. Tough loss, they win this handily if the Megrim isn't countered.

NLFY. Skate to victory in this one. Had 2x Black Knight and eventually a Sengir to get it done. Nearly a no-contest win. A big one in the standings though, as NLFY gains a 1-game lead on MM to put some pressure on first place (Sliver & Folk Music can still keep a lead w/ Ws).

MM. Very fun to see 2x Gauntlet of Might out there but in practice it didn't seem to make any sense with a "weenie" deck. There was no way to take advantage of the mana surplus, made me revert back to the DnS + Goblins then mana surplus of Gauntlet of Might + Mana Flare for the Fire Drilled concept. More play-testing required!

Slivers. Jesus H. Christ, these Slivers, man.. Took out a T-4 Dreadnought via Transmute Artifact then threw 7x Slivers down to chip away, with a Coat of Arms joining on T-7 for a MASSIVE 38x damage to claim the dub. Goddamn.

Overdrawn. I mean, they have this game WRAPPED UP if not for the fortuitous Swords to Plowshares. Of course, Slivers had another one in-hand as well. Tough loss here for a team searching for some momentum at now 3-8.

X. Looked real good in this one - just the 3x mana for most of the game, but a Sol Ring enabled them to put 2x Rukh Egg, a Lhurgoyf into play beside an early Ape, then a late Cataclsym wiped out DDs whole situation. Fourth win in a row for X!

Doom. Good mana base but didn’t have any early creatures to summon for offense / defense. They sort of sat by with a Force of Nature, 2x Call of the Wild and a Hurricane in hand with nowhere to go.

DnS. Damn son! By turn-4, Stormy had a Two-headed Giant and a Furnace of Rath on the field - with three quick smoke makers in hand - to the tune of 8x damage, then another 22x damage the next turn. Furnace of Rath + DD spells really shining here. Also sat on a Manabarbs in hand that would’ve chipped away all game at what is a slow squad in YCBI.

YCBI. Had one mana and nothing else, total mulligan for them, but it wasn’t looking good!!!

Music. Run away with is one after turning Maze of Ith into an Island, though Lord of Atlantis’ Islandwalk ability was perhaps the bigger game changer - kind of a tough draw for the Freaks.

Freaks. What can they do against Islandwalk? Brutal.

Machine. BAF only had 2x lands, no fighters out there so they couldn't win their game w/o fighters. Similarly, the Machine had a quick Su-Chi & Phyrexian War Beast to dole out 3-turns of 7x damage for the W.

BAF. Smoked w/o any additional lands to defend themselves - now their 5th loss in a row!!!

T-10. Lucky victory with only 3x lands. Drawing Yawgmoth Demon w/ several artifacts in play made all the difference. Me and Dad can't believe it! 

The Atts. They had a close game, probably the second MVP in Necropotence, says Colten - a very powerful card. Had the early lead but lost several creatures in battle against 2x Elvish Archers + 'Ol Yawggy.

Things. Goddamn!! One of the games of the year! Things are drilled down to a single life and counter Cleaning on turn-7 to stay alive. On turn-8, they draw a second Swamp unleashing a Living Death for 2x Kraken, El Capitaino & Crimson Hellkite for a victory on turn-9!

Potatoes. Nearly a masterclass in control here, as Cleansing would've forced the Things to destroy all lands thus disabling the next turn Living Death. Heck of a game!

Machine. Oh baby, it's working! They out-speed the #1 seed with 4x Goblin, including a Recruiter to tutor 4x Goblin King - only two made it out and that was enough to grab the W.

Sliver. Not often they get outgunned like this! Couldn't play some mountains or the 2x Coat of Arms in-hand given the Mountainwalk + gang abilities of their opponent. Fun dynamic between these two.

NLFY. Second victory against X, and their 4th in a row, this time taking hold of the #1 seed. NLFY gained an edge on a turn-2 Hymn to Tourach, destroying the first 5 mana producers. NLFYs card advantage crew til it was insurmountable with a Black Knight and Sengir Vampire out for blood!

X. End of a 5-game win streak! Still really love the Survival of the Fittest + Lhurgoyf combination, and it was right there again this game - X just couldn't find any footing after the early hole.

OD. Well well, there's life in this group after all. Prevailing thought after watching this masterpiece was that I just love this combination of cards; Winds of Change into Underworld Dreams -> Illusionary Mask -> Demonic Tutor -> Dreadnought + Lord of Tresserhorn. Just awesome to see it all. Laid down 32 damage in a single turn, too. Not bad.

YCBI. Sat on a single land; mulligan! Had 2x Counterspell in hand too, so could've potentially held off the onslaught.

Stormy. 4-turn win, damn. Not many of those floating around! Cool thing about this game was that the W was in sight from the initial draw w/ Bolt, Incinerate, Pyrokinesis in hand - they sat on 10x damage, then drew the second Pyrokinesis and the final 6x damage in Ball Lightning. Pretty sick win.

Doom. Man, they were all set up to go on the offensive with a 2x Sylvan Library + Call of the Wild combo in play and 2x Verdant Force at the top of the library w/ Kaysa to follow. Game was in-sight but DnS was just too fast. Fourth loss in a row for DD.

Potatoes. Back at it here, the Potatoes seemed to be in control the entire game w/ a Crusade<>Kjeldoran Outpost in play and a Land Tax as a fallback control element. Tundra Wolves did most of the work, but it was Crusade that truly enabled the victory.

Music. Ends a 5-game win streak. Never got going in this one really. The Merfolk they summoned were powerless without their team captain. A Seasinger could’ve turned things around in their favor early, but it wouldn’t have mattered much in the longer term.

Attritionists. Looked good here on very little man.. this is the kind of game a weenie deck can win where they sit on mainly 2-mana for the majority of the game and still kick out 3x creatures and a Bad Moon. They were uncontested here and cruised to victory.

Things. Mana shortage crushed their meaty aspirations!!!

Team 10. They were lucky that BAF had mostly all lands and barely any fighters - especially with Winter Orb (MVP) in play. The Black Vise + Winter Orb combo was effective and BAF didn’t really stand a chance.

BAF. Sixth loss in a row! Had too many lands and barely any fighters which gave Team 10 a chance to win with Winter Orb + Black Vise in play. Couldn’t fight, and didn’t last long as a result!

Freaks. Really fun game for them actually, controlled the whole thing nearly start-to-finish. Most of their damage was via a stolen Juggernaut w/ Unstable Mutation. They countered a potentially game-changing Xanthic Statue and Field of Dreams added a really fun element - they mill’ed several times to keep mana from being drawn. Flying also played a role in this game.

Machine. Real tough loss, felt like they were right there for much of the game - kept drawing baddies to throw out there but couldn’t get a foothold. Nevinyrral’s Disk was huge in wiping the board, paving the way for Xanthic Statue but it was countered - changed the entire game.

Machine. The Goblin Recruiter.. dude just puts his fist up and the swarm assembles. He helps bring 2x King onto the battlefield - in addition to 1x King already in-hand - and completely demolish the YCBI defenses. They finished this in style too, using a Goblin Grenade + Fork to deal the finishing blow. Great win.

YCBI. Continue to struggle here, losing a game they should’ve lost frankly, and yet with a bit more mana could’ve made this more interesting. They should’ve had ~4 mana on 14 cards but had only three, and I think that hurt them considerably.

X. This deck is so fun and the recent inclusion of Survival of the Fittest continues to make waves. They are happy to lose creatures - keen on it at times - to power the insatiable Lhurgoyf. The synergy was on in this game and when it’s against DnS, man, they’ll take all the direct-damage-killing-creatures action they can get. MVP was tough to pin down, but probably Angelic Renewal on a Wrath of God saving Birds of Paradise to summon the third and final Lhurgoyf to bring home the dub.

DnS. Fall right into Xs hands unfortunately - probably not the first time I’ve written that either. Still, had the game winning combo in-hand to start w/ 2x Furnace of Rath and several bolts. Didn’t get there because of a Disenchant but the W was in sight.

Sliver. They had so many great guys - the lands helped them, so many defenders, Dad said “Hurricane could’ve killed all the Sliver Lining defenders” but after drawing five cards the Doom didn’t find it which gave Sliver Lining the win. Having 5x Sliver out there w/ Coat of Arms on 5-turns for a whopping 35-damage on the final turn is absurd.

Doom. I mean, ridiculous. They barely had time to get set up and it was over. The Worldly Tutor <> Call of the Wild is fun - but slow. They don’t really have any early defense against a weenie team like this - used to put a Woolly Spider out there but no longer. Interesting development.

NLFY. Destroy the first four lands played totally disabling Overdrawn. If not the fortuitous string of M-Kill cards, OD would’ve been able to throw a Mask-Tresserhorn combo into play and likely won the game. NLFY marches on in sole possession of first place!

OD. This team is super fun and would likely have won the game if not for a ridiculous string of M-Kill cards. Unlucky, I say!!

Folk Music. Lucky to have won by 5 points, played several cards that changed the game in their favor including a Force Spike on Grave Pact early on. Seasinger stole a Hidden Horror, and then Lord of Atlantis took full control of the board to swing the advantage.

Attritionists. They had a little bit of an early lead and by the end they almost had it but Folk Music dealt 8-damage to win the game. Still looked good in this one, though did have some clunkiness early on what to play and when w/ HIdden Horror, Fallen Angel, Bad Moon and Order of the Ebon Hand.

Fault. Benefitted from a slow start for the Potatoes, but otherwise put on a clinic of control by destroying lands and discarding cards. Bottomless Pit ended up winning this game w/ The Rack which is a win condition I didn’t plan on much. No creature offense really, and 2x Megrim were disenchanted along the way. So it was a longer, slower game but there was enough M-Kill to keep a Land Tax at bay while the two discard engines reduced Potatoes’ moves to rubble.

Potatoes. Land Tax is annoying AF 😆 but never got going here because of the aforementioned forces at work.

Things. It’s an interesting win here, because they drew 3x fewer lands yet were able to deploy twice as many cards (12 v 6). They parlayed a Lim Dul’s Vault into Demonic Tutor into Living Death into Spirit of the Night w/ 2x Unstable Mutation. They’re almost never out of it if they have some draw cards.

Freaks. Had nothing going in this game on either side of the ball.. two counters were nullified by a Counterspell then simply paying the mana tax for Mana Leak. Kind of a “nothing doing” game.

T-10. Incredibly unusual game where Wil-o-the-Wisp attacks unblocked w/ 2x Unholy Strength attached for 4x damage per shot. Black Vise did a turn or two of damage, and then the Relic Barrier acted as perfect foil to the all-artifact opponent, stifling an early Juggernaut from pummeling their defenses.

Facts Machine. Tough loss, they were swimming in mana and had nothing to summon.

Machine. What a banger! They threw the literal gauntlet down in what was a calculated risk - they would win next turn thanks to a Fireball in-hand and essentially gambled that 20 remaining life would be enough in case DnS threw some heat their way. All 3x goblins went down on the ensuing turn, but they walked out w/ a flare dealing 20x damage on the final turn for the W.

DnS. I mean this was RIGHT there for them. Gauntlet of Might -> Furnace of Rath had HAYMAKERS flying in from all directions. They win next turn if Mean Machine doesn’t counterpunch. What a fun game of mono-red enemies!

Doom. First time I’ve seen Verdant Force in play - not to mention 2x of them!!! The Worldly Tutor -> Call of the Wild is very fun. There was a 1/1 Sapling token + Overrun win condition on the way until Wrath of God hit. Cool to see this in action, and a needed win for the Doom!

YCBI. Falter yet again despite wiping out a Verdant Force + 5x Sapling tokens. No real offense beyond the 1/1 peckers and yet had a 5/5 Angel in hand.

X. One of their best games of the season. OD shuffled hands several times and it didn’t seem to matter - good mana gave way to 2x Kird Ape, 2x Lightning Elemental and a River Boa to win the old fashioned way. They were fairly untested - not much defense or offense from OD, but maybe it wouldn’t have mattered. X wins easy.

OD. This is the “bust” version of this squad.. draw, draw some more, shuffle, shuffle again and.. nothing. Finally swung a Nought toward the end but it was far too late and was countered via Disenchant immediately. Not a great game for OD.

NLFY. What a clinic - their sixth in a row! The #1 seed vs #2 seed, and they just stifled every single sliver  (eh?) of momentum. They took out 6x lands, relied on 2x Black Vise for much of the early rounds damage, then squashed a mid-game attack by 2x Muscle Sliver via N-Disk, then put ‘em away with Time Bomb late game. Just masterful stuff by NLFY.

Sliver. How do you win a game when 6x lands are destroyed? They had the mix - 2x Muscle, 2x Heart, Talon, Sliver Queen & a Coat of Arms - but could never get going thanks to ridiculous defensive moves by the All Blacks. They’ll want another run at NLFY this season!

Folk. I mean, 2x Island and they completely own the game. Tidal Warrior takes MVP honors, enabling the Merfolk of the Pearl Trident + Lord of Atlantis to attack unencumbered. Once the Lord came out it was over. Dandan was also great - 4/1 for 2x Island is quite a deal. They cruise into 2nd place with this victory.

Team 10. Tough sledding here, they put ‘Ol Yawggy out to play but he was countered immediately. It still may not have changed the game too much give Folk’s lead. They raced out early, too, with what I’ve found to be a sneaky good combo; Elvish Archers + Unholy Strength. A 4/2 w/ first strike for 3-mana.. not bad at all.

DnS. Ridiculous and historic victory in only 3-turns. Here’s how they did it:

1st turn: Mountain + Sol Ring
2nd turn: Mountain + Furnace of Rath
3rd turn: Mountain + Ball Lightning + Pyrokinesis for 20x damage

Swamp Things. Opening hand had their deadly combo - would likely win 3-turns later.

Lining. I mean, what is anyone supposed to do against 4x dual lands and City of Brass with slivers and Coat of Arms? Nothing, that’s what. Total beat down.

Potatoes. Looked sluggish and woefully unprepared to handle the speed from Slivers.

YCBI. Oh yeah!! Break a 6-game losing streak in a tight one. Meekstone + Stasis really won this one, all but disabling the opposing Machine. They get control of the board, can peck away and wait for the Angel brigade to bring the rain. Pretty cool!

Machine. Meekstone took all the wind out of their sails with really no way to deal damage outside of 2/2 creatures.

Music. Draw 3x Lord of Atlantis to win the game - two of them hastened via Arcane Denial draw advantage. What a fortuitous happening to keep sole possession of 2nd place.

OD. I mean, this sucks. They countered 1x Lord only to hasten the summoning of 2x more? AYFKM? Unreal, and yet they put 2x Nought on the field too. Just unlucky here.

Doom. Beautiful beautiful stuff in this game. Synergies galore w/ Worldly Tutor -> Verdant Force via Call of the Wild. Then, Waiting in the Weeds for 8x Cat tokens in addition to the 5x Sapling’s already in play. I’ve never seen this strategy play out before and it was stampeding in this one. So so fun!

Team 10. Looked sluggish, though had 3x Vise in play that would’ve wrecked just about any other team unable to deploy like Darwin.

Freaks. Had a great game all because of Propaganda - it held Mean Machine back because they didn’t have enough mana to attack. Propaganda gave them the win, and it gave them lots of cards too, which had Mean Machine lost. Equilibrium didn’t play much of a role, though Control Magic stole a critical Goblin King mid-game as well - further turning the tide.

Mean Machine. Had the early lead thanks to Ball Lightning, though lost themselves after stacking an entire horde of Goblins via Goblin Recruiter - they never drew another land and were stuck bc of Propaganda.

Attritionists. BIGGEST WIN OF THE YEAR, taking down the #1 seed in style! 2x Bad Moon w/ pumped Carnophage and other creatures kicked up the pace and I don’t remember any other details!

NLFY. The 7-game win streak is over! What a run, and to go down to a fellow mono-black squad. They retain a 1-game lead in the standings.

DnS. Came down to the last card, needed any card to deal 2 or more damage for less than 4 CMC and they got it - Ball Lightning!!! Unreal and super fun. 

Bad Ass Fault. Crawled back into the game thanks to 2x Bottomless Pit and shined a light on the need for The Rack in this game. It was interesting to see so much discard, they didn’t use much M-Kill.

Lining. Two major breaks in this game; (1) drawing a Disenchant to destroy a resurrected Polar Kraken via Necromancy, then (2) seeing the Things draw an Island instead of a Swamp thus negating a Living Death play. Otherwise, they deal 30x damage in only 5-turns thanks to Savannah / Plateau and 2x Muscle, Heart & Talon Sliver.

Things. Mana had them stuck, forcing early discard (not the worst thing) and general fizzled play.

Machine. Wow! Lotus Petal plays a huge role - drew 3x to start and quickly got a Triskelion in play. Sol Ring enables Su-Chi, Mindless Automaton and Tetravus to get out there and lay mechanical waste to newly revised Hand Grenade - fun game and they were MUCH faster!

Grenade. Well, it was fun to see direct damage + U-Dreams, their opponent was just too fast. Without the Lotus Petals this likely goes to Grenade. Fun!

Music. Just a smooth operator with a single Tidal Warrior + Lord of Atlantis doing all the damage. They couldn’t be blocked with a Phantasmal Terrain out there- really a lesson to the other weenie decks that landwalk abilities are wildly underrated. Their fourth win in a row has them tied for 1st place w/ NLFY!

Doom. Close to a mulligan game with no real momentum whatsoever. It was interesting that they had two lands-turned-to-islands, a Fyndhorn Elves and Gaea’s Touch and couldn’t really get anywhere. A Kaysa, 2x Call of the Wild, Overrun, Y-Ants… more high-CMC cards than I would’ve expected. Would they be better off trading some of the high-CMCs for low? Maybe.

Machine. Win on only 2x lands in play which not many teams can do. Colten, “Kind of a lucky game, but Team 10 had great cards like Winter Orb-Black Vise-Ankh of Mishra in play.. so just a lucky game for Mean Machine.”

Team 10. As Colten rightfully mentioned above, they had their soft-lock in play and were cruising along roughly 2-turns from winning. They had lots of lands and, at the end, I thought Worldly Tutor could swing the game with Birds of Paradise which ended up giving MM the game.”

TTFs. First game looks mighty good. An insane starting hand w/ Sol Ring, Dreadnought and then drawing Mask on turn-1. They also had a key Counterspell to fizzle a Disenchant. There’s something about the artifact + blue that’s easy on the eyes, too.

X. I mean, nobody sticking around w/ that battlefield. Unlucky.

DnS. Cold as ice in this one, doling out hits left and right, and just in time too because The Atts would've won a turn later!!

Atts. Ya know, they looked pretty smooth here. First time I'd seen Lake of the Dead w/ this crew, and it enabled a Dark Ritual -> Bad Moon + Sengir Autocrat. The Atts did 10x a turn later. Very fun!

Sliver. Shot out of a canon in this game - when they draw ample mana they can summon almost anything,and that was the case in this one. A Heart Sliver, 2x Muscle, 1x Victual Sliver, 1x Crystalline Sliver. Game was over in what felt like minutes.

BAF. Mana struggles decimated their chances. They need to draw M-Kill almost immediately to survive against this squad, no dice.

YCBI. O-Kay YCBI! Drew on 2x Counterspell and a 1x Disenchant to protect themselves and pave the way for 3x Falcon to do their thing. Get walloped if they dont have the removal defense.

Things. Man, drop their fourth in a row sliding to 8-9 record. This was a tough matchup for them. Were a bit slow out the gate due to mana shortages but still win this game handily without removal spells.

Grenade. Ended a 4-game losing streak!! Super important game - their second as a new unit - showcased the power of excessive draw power + Underworld Dreams. Looked like a real team. Colten’s note, “They got a lucky play in the middle of the game where they put U-Dreams out and made a lot of players in his hand go right into the game and attack. We also brought Two-headed Giant of Foriys which was critical. But Teferi’s Puzzle Box came out twice which made the Potatoes draw six cards - TWICE - and gave Hand Grenade the win, a great win, and a big upset.”

Potatoes. Had this game won with a busy battlefield - 1x White Knight, 1x Kjeldoran Home Guard, 2x Knight tokens, 1x Tundra Wolves - but couldn’t make any ground because of the Giant.

Machine. Fairly unopposed in this game. Threw 2x Juggernaut, 1x Tetravus onto the field and attacked at-will! Now two wins in a row for Facts Machine! Lotus Petal helping out in what seems like game after game. Good addition.

Doom. Tough initial hand w/ cards they couldn’t use (Force of Nature, Verdant Force) and only 1-mana. They were playing from very far behind from the get go.

Machine. Colten, “MM had a game-winner against #2 seed Folk Music. Folk Music just had so many lands that it gave MM a great chance of getting Goblin King out, and they also killed Seasinger + Lord of Atlantis which gave MM the win.” Tight game, battle of two top seeds (#2 v #5) and an early Goblin Recruiter secured 3x Goblin King to be drawn-and-played. Some timely direct damage took care of 2x Lord of Atlantis and a troubling Seasinger.

Music. Colten, “Had way too many lands, hung down to 1-card and MM got so many great players and had a great game and had Folk Music lost in the music!”

X. Make it four-in-a-row! Slow-rolling game, but were able to outlast a Orb-Vice-Ankh combo thanks to Survival of the Fittest and Wrath of God. Lhurgoyf did the rest!

Team 10. That’s now four losses in a row. More and more I am disliking the control style of play. It’s just not as fun to lock someone up w/ neither side able to play anything. It’s torturous!

NLFY. I mean, clockwork? Kill 2x lands, destroy a Serendib Efreet, put a Sengir and Hyppy out there. Game over!

Treat Freaks. Had the first turn Sol Ring -> Mask combo in hand, but never drew the ‘Nought.

Attritionists. Yeahhhhh baby, showing they belong with THE top dog of the past few seasons. In a way, they were built to annoy the F out of the Slivers and did just that in this game. 

Slivers. Drew a lot of mana, leaving them light on the creature offensive. They couldn’t outlast the sac engine of the Atts, tough game.

BAF. Pretty sweet, chip away with The Rack - card dealt the lion’s share of damage in this game - and ended things with a wonderfully timely Pox, knocking YCBI life to 1, then forcing a discard for 2x more damage with Megrim. Ya love to see it!

YCBI. Didn’t have much in this game but stuck around and put light pressure on to make the Fault uncomfortable.

Things. Break a 4-game losing streak - big one. Had a Kraken out on turn-3 via Buried Alive, game over two turns later as a second Necromancy sent a Spirit of the Night out onto the ‘field as well.

Grenade. This is the Mr. Hyde-side of Grenade, where they sit there shuffling hand after hand with nothing to show for it.

Potatoes. Armageddon puts Darwin on its heels, inflecting 8x damage per turn. A Crusade was key also, pumping Wolves and Order of Leitbur to take ‘em home.

Doom. Win this game without the big guy in play. First time I’d seen Pendelhaven out there - beautiful art in the card! Also further reinforced that Waiting in the Weeds should be reduced for more Elves most likely.

MM. Damn Blood Moon! Totally covered his costs by completely nullifying Facts Machine mana base. A Recruiter pulled in the march of the Kings, and that was that! Mean Machine slides into 2nd place with the W.

Facts Machine. Would’ve been able to trade haymakers with the goblin horde had it not been for Blood Moon. Instead, they pretty much put one foot in front of the other - especially when Goblin King came out and Mountainwalk took over. Pretty rad combo.

TT. So this is the first game after another swap of cards (3x Serendib Efreet <> Mystical Tutor, Mana Drain) and though it didn’t play a distinct role in the game, you could see the clarity with which Trick or Treat Freaks operated. They used a turn-1 Tutor to grab Transmute Artifact - an insurance play should they come upon a Millstone or Scroll Rack. The shuffle produced an Illusionary Mask a few turns later, and it was over before it really started. It’ll be interesting to see if tutoring like hell works - or if it ends up failing in the gameplay category. 

DnS. 4x Bolts and a Ball Lightning. Had they drawn another Mountain earlier they could’ve summoned their 2nd Ball Lightning and possibly won the game. This is just about as fast as they can go - bringing TTFs down to 2-life in only five turns. Tough loss.

Atts. Sure, YCBI did absolutely nothing in this game, but there’s still something to be said about the Atts deploying 6x cards on only 3x lands. I even felt like they should have had more creatures in this game, and when Kormus Bell arrived I didn’t feel great about it. There’s something vulnerable about all lands being creatures, especially when playing a team that has even one Wrath of God.

YCBI. Stink fest, drew 8 lands on 14 cards.

Grenade. A ridiculous game in which HG plays 10 cards, loses a bunch more thanks to 4x M-Kill cards and a host of discard - YET, they draw a fortuitous Giant who wins the game late. They were on their last legs and somehow had enough mana after 11-turns to put that guy in play. An absurd finish.

BAF. Looked dominant in this game yet bumped into a funny reality - they had wrecked HGs hand, yet, there was little else they could do despite a Bottomless Pit and 2x Megrim in play. What an odd position to be in, almost like they needed another win condition..

Doom. Big shift in strategy ahead of this game - replace the Call of the Wild <> Sylvan Library <> Big Boys for land destruction cards (Thermokarst, Ice Shield, Kudzu) and lower CMC creatures (eg Elvish Ranger). Killed off 2x lands in this one, win handily and break a 4-game losing streak in the process! I think there’s something sustainable to build on with this M-Kill strategy that leans elf/tribal.

Things. Didn’t have much jump in this one, namely bc of the mana loss.

Facts Machine. Urzatron kick-starts some offense for the Machine, tossing a Triskelion, Su-Chi and 2x Mishra’s Factory out to attack unencumbered.

X. Stuck on three lands generating W/R w/ a hand full of green cards!

NLFY. Wow, first time I’ve seen a game like this— Cyclopean Tomb gets ahead of the opposing mana ramp and completely disables any card deployment by the Music. NLFY marched ahead a second thought, powered by Black Knight, Hypnotic Specter, 2x Vampire. Pretty cool win.

Music. Had their first land destroyed by Strip Mine and once they fell behind Cyclopean Tomb it was over. Wild!

T-10. Well, well, just as they’re on the brink of deprecation they pull out a rare 4-turn win! And against the fireworks display that is Dark’n Stormy! Wow! Their opening hand was super fun.. a Swamp -> Sol Ring -> Felwar Stone -> Black Vise. Next turn, Ankh of Mishra. 3x Mishra Factory took the baton from there and that was that! A Furnace of Rath sped things up a bit ;)

DnS. May not have played this correctly, using a Chain Lightning + Incinerate early instead of waiting to pair w/ Fork and/or Furnace of Rath. They win on the next turn too, ironically.

TTFs. Wild game for this team, showcasing everything good and bad about the mono-blue combo-hopeful! They shuffled cards early, using Scroll Rack to constantly get sneak peaks on the library to finally pull a Transmute Artifact that sent a Millstone out for an Illusionary Mask - 2x Nought followed and that was that!

Slivers. Sick dual land draw but were without R and left four cards stranded in-hand for most of the game.

Doom. Survived losing a handful of lands namely due to their Elf power — 2x Fyndhorn in play with more in-hand meant they could withstand BAFs initial attack. Pretty well balanced for this type of opponent.

BAF. Feeling out of sorts a bit here, didn’t have much in the way of creature defense to handle the Yavimaya Ants.

Mean Machine. Well, this team continues to steamroll. Super fun to see Blood Moon move the needle, pairing nicely with Goblin King to attack unimpeded against the Things. There’s not really any other dimension to this team which could cause problems down the road, but for now they just kind of swarm and do their thing.

Things. Too slow for the squad despite having a 1-turn Hidden Horror w/ 2-turn Unstable Mutation.

Potatoes. WILD game, highlighted Potatoes’ ability to go small and control late. Land Tax won them the game, what after a Cataclysm left them w/ a Tundra Wolves facing off with a double-digit Lhurgoyf, they effectively sacrificed the Wolves and played Balance next turn to kill off the big guy. Huge play, used Land Tax again later with Armageddon nullifying X attack. Wild game.

X. Have a funny situation where something like Lightning Elemental & there other big green guy have almost no utility in a game like this. Elementals will get killed by 1/1 Wolves w/ first strike and the big guy costs too much mana. Wonder if they need a killer - like Royal Assassin - to clear the way.

DnS. Fascinating game, #7 takes down #3! Manabarbs played a key role, disincentivizing Folk Music from deploying almost anything - instead playing it close to the vest. Dark n Stormy played it slow, waiting for the last DD spell to arrive to increase win probability.

Folk Music. Lost a Lord of Atlantis early and sat on mostly unhelpful cards the rest of the way. 2x Phantasmal Terrain were helpful, as was a Maze of Ith (kept Ball Lightning from ending things even earlier).

Slivers. Is this the turnaround they’ve been looking for?! They started out in the hole - down to 8-life before they inflicted a point of damage - thanks to 2x Black Vise and a Winter Orb. But this squad is capable of deploying w/ the best of them and, hey, they kept drawing lands en route to 2x Muscle Sliver, 1x Metallic Sliver and 1x Heart Sliver.

Team 10. Had an angle early on the 2x Vise w/ a Stone-throwing Devil, Elvish Archer + Unholy Strength in hand but the Swamp never came! One thing I find challenging with this group is they want to win on Black Vise — and so does NLFY.. it might be more fun to see a different win-con surface.

TTFs. Man, look at this thing.. opening hand includes 1x Nought, a Mystical Tutor to grab Transmute Artifact turn-1, then Scroll Rack played on turn-2 - quickly disenchanted - but nonetheless followed by an Illusionary Mask. The influx of Scroll Rack + more Transmute Artifact + Mystical Tutor suddenly enable much faster searching and more ways to find the combo as opposed to the endless shuffling of Big Gloves. Pretty cool.

YCBI. So big, clunky and slow. Kismet as a tentpole card is tough at 4 CMC - harkens back to learning this lesson w/ Portcullis, Pandemonium and others in that same category. Stasis sucks also, it makes sense in here but has got to go on playability alone.

Machine. Win streak extends to 5 as they put pressure on #1-NLFY, now up a game in the win column! Sluggish start for both sides, gained an edge simply on drawing two more lands. Of note; it feels like Gauntlet of Might at 4 CMC is a LOT to summon and, come to think of it, I’m not sure I’ve ever seen it hit the battlefield! Also, it deserves to be in a Fire Drilled type construction, right?

BAF. Drew only 1x land, not going to beat anyone in that situation.

X. A really nice win in an unconventional way, using Survival of the Fittest to pull Lightning Elemental out for faster attack - 3x Elemental manned the battlefield and ended things quickly for X. Fun win!

Things. Tempted to say they were too slow, and maybe they were, but I also think they just need a bit more time to get going usually.

NLFY. Strong win, held the advantage pretty much the entire game. Cyclopean Tomb was demonstrative, flipping 4x lands into Swamps, while Black Vise, Black Knight do the lion’s share of the work.

Potatoes. Critical mistake in this game was casting White Knight instead of playing Balance. They also should’ve wiped all mana w/ Armageddon and leaned on Land Tax - big risk, but could’ve worked.

Facts Machine. Wow!! Turn-1 Urza’s Mine + Lotus Pedal -> 2x Mana Vault -> Triskelion. That is some SHIT RIGHT THERE! Sure, he was killed two turns later, but the Urzatron formed and pushed a Tetravus + Xanthic Statue onto the field for the unimpeded W. What, a, win.

DnS. Interesting cards drawn in this game; 5 lands, 2x Furnace of Rath, Manabarbs and some DD. Makes me feel like you can really only play Manabarbs and/or Furnace of Rath if you’re stocked with fireworks - it’s hard to play one of them just to sit there.

Sliver. Put 4x slivers out on 3x lands and cruise to victory. This was more of a "No Contest" victory than anything else.

Music. Losing in 4-turns in only possible against a few squads - obviously this is one of them. Stuck on 2x mana, they couldn't get any momentum going. May have played them incorrectly as well.

YCBI. Somewhat untouched in this game thanks to early deployment - they got out from under a Black Vise and chipped away with 2x Peckers, then a Serra Angel later on. Nothing fancy, just marched to victory.

Team 10. Looked clunky and slow in this game. Got the Orb-Vise-Relic Barrier combo out there but by then it was a bit too late. The Elvish Archers and Stone-throwing Devils didn’t do much and couldn’t defend the fliers.

TTFs. Alright, lots happening in this game. First of all, the TTFs just making straight up music out there! Field of Dreams to watch the deck, grab a Mask, use Copy Artifact on a Nought, then a Transmute Artifact on a Millstone just for extra effect. I mean goddamn, the tutoring in this deck is much refined from last season’s incessant shuffling.

Hand Grenade. Alright there’s something powerful in here, but the deck needs Relic Barrier & Icy Manipulator — then they can paralyze the opponent while protecting themselves and gaining draw advantage w/ Howling Mine. I think Underworld Dreams is still in here, maybe with Royal Assassin (w/ Icy), Entropic Spector (w/ Mine) and maybe Kormus Bell.

The Atts. Magnificent game, leveraging the strengths of MVP - Lake of the Dead - to put maximum pressure on Mean Machine. Bad Moon played an integral role in the W, accelerating damage every single turn w/ 2x Nether Shadow.

Mean Machine. Loved seeing the 2x Gauntlet of Might out there (!) but they only drew a single creature all game to benefit. Seems like Gauntlet of Might does not fit in this deck 🤷🏻‍♂️

X. So, it’s becoming increasingly clear that Survival of the Fittest + Haste is huge for this team. I find that Lightning Elemental gets the nod in some situations (no creature defense) whereas Lhurgoyf gets the nod when there is creature defense. Fascinating finding, and suggests possibly that X should feature another creature or two w/ Haste.

BAF. An interesting loss for them, as they drew a good deal of mana (5x lands) but had a mismatch of Mountains to predominantly Black hand. There’s probably more that can be done to help with mana creation on this team and it should be explored asap.

Things. O-K Things! Drew 4x lands, had 2x destroyed and still pummeled NLFY thanks to a turn-1 Hidden Horror and Unstable Mutation soon-to-follow. They did have a full graveyard and sat on 2x Living Death so it’s not as though they were without other moves. Looked good here, nice win to close out the season.

NLFY. Tough end to the season, sitting on 2x Terror yet unable to cast thanks to opposing black creatures!

Potatoes. Still at it, turn the tide and totally own the pace of the game w/ multiple M-Kill cards in hand. Kjeldoran Outpost was big, too.

DnS. Threw a handful of DD spells out, handled most of the early attackers with Pyroclasm + Pyrokinesis but ran out of gas. The loss of 4x lands mid-game crushed them too, after having just played a Furnace of Rath - it would’ve paved the way to a W.

Sliver. On 3x lands, they deployed 6+ creatures and drew just enough defense to hold off Facts Machine (ie Xanthic Statue). Tetravus, specifically, came out on turn-2 and would’ve attacked unfettered if not for a timely Disenchant.

Machine. Real close, win if Tetravus doesn’t get killed, needed another creature or two. The Hive stinks.

Music. Lord of Atlantis + Islandwalk + opponent splashing blue = OVER! No contest.

YCBI. Terrible team, excited to stop playing with them :)

Team 10. Nothing spectacular here, creatures vs no creatures (save a Shocker). There's just something about a black and green deck that pleases the eye!!!

Grenade. Drew 3x U-Dreams but couldn't play 'em. Still, can't build an entire team around 4 cards.

Doom. A FUCKING AWESOME game for the doom-squad. This team has been struggling to find it's identity all season and this current iteration is the most fun yet.. teaming Call of the Wild w/ 2x Sylvan Library + Worldly Tutor is a synergy at its finest. This game the combos pulled a Verdant Force, Force of Nature and Yavimaya Ants onto the battlefield. Two Saproling tokens were on the field, too, helped by Pendelhaven + Heart of Yavimaya. Super fun and put up 29 damage in a hurry. Lets go baby!

TTFs. Total mulligan game, drew a single land in 14 cards. This team needs more Mystical Tutors. Oh well.
